input.content:
# Journey: Resource Server Verification

## Overview

The complete flow when a resource server receives a request bearing a DPoP-bound access token. The resource server must verify token validity, sender binding (proof-of-possession), action scope (RAR authorization_details), and replay protection before executing the requested action.

## Actors

- **Agent Runtime** (code_agent "Kira" — the token presenter)
- **Resource Server** (Brain API endpoint or external integration proxy)
- **Nonce Cache** (time-windowed set for DPoP replay protection)

## Emotional Arc

```
Start: Vigilant          Middle: Methodical           End: Assured
  |                          |                           |
  v                          v                           v
"Every request is       "Each check has a          "All checks passed --
 potentially hostile"    clear pass/fail"           request is legitimate"
```

## Verification Pipeline

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  INCOMING REQUEST                                                 |
|                                                                    |
|  POST /api/integrations/stripe/invoices                            |
|  Authorization: DPoP eyJhbGciOiJFUzI1NiIs...  (access token)      |
|  DPoP: eyJ0eXAiOiJkcG9wK2p3dCIs...            (proof JWT)         |
|  Content-Type: application/json                                    |
|  { customer: "cus_acme_corp", amount: 240000, currency: "usd" }   |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 1: Header Extraction                                        |
|  Emotion: Vigilant -- "parse before trusting"                      |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Extract from request:                                             |
|    1. Authorization header -> token_type + access_token            |
|       - MUST be "DPoP <token>" (not "Bearer <token>")             |
|       - If "Bearer" with a DPoP-bound token -> 401                |
|    2. DPoP header -> dpop_proof (JWT)                              |
|       - MUST be present when token_type is DPoP                   |
|       - Missing DPoP header -> 401                                 |
|                                                                    |
|  Fail-fast: missing or malformed headers -> 401 immediately        |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 2: Access Token Validation                                  |
|  Emotion: Methodical -- "standard JWT verification"                |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Validate the access token JWT:                                    |
|    1. Signature verification via JWKS (existing flow)              |
|    2. Issuer: "https://brain.example/api/auth"                     |
|    3. Audience: "https://brain.example"                            |
|    4. Expiration: exp > now (with clock tolerance)                 |
|    5. Required claims present: sub, cnf.jkt,                       |
|       authorization_details, urn:brain:workspace                   |
|                                                                    |
|  Extract for downstream checks:                                    |
|    ${cnf_jkt} = token.cnf.jkt                                      |
|    ${authorization_details} = token.authorization_details           |
|    ${workspace_id} = token["urn:brain:workspace"]                   |
|    ${intent_id} = token["urn:brain:intent_id"]                     |
|                                                                    |
|  This step reuses existing createJwtValidator from                  |
|  mcp/token-validation.ts with extended claim requirements.          |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 3: DPoP Proof Validation                                    |
|  Emotion: Vigilant -- "this is the sender-constraining check"      |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Validate the DPoP proof JWT:                                      |
|                                                                    |
|  3a. Structure check:                                              |
|    - Header typ MUST be "dpop+jwt"                                 |
|    - Header alg MUST be an approved asymmetric algorithm (ES256)   |
|    - Header jwk MUST contain the public key                        |
|                                                                    |
|  3b. Signature verification:                                       |
|    - Verify proof signature using the embedded JWK public key      |
|                                                                    |
|  3c. Claims validation:                                            |
|    - htm (HTTP method) MUST match the request method ("POST")      |
|    - htu (HTTP URI) MUST match the request URI                     |
|    - iat (issued at) MUST be within acceptable window              |
|      (e.g., now - 60s <= iat <= now + 5s for clock skew)           |
|    - jti (unique identifier) MUST be present                       |
|                                                                    |
|  3d. Replay protection:                                            |
|    - Check jti against the nonce cache                             |
|    - If jti already seen -> 401 "dpop_proof_reused"                |
|    - If jti is fresh -> add to nonce cache with TTL                |
|                                                                    |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|  | Nonce Cache (time-windowed set)                             |   |
|  |------------------------------------------------------------|   |
|  | jti: "abc123" | expires: now + 5min                         |   |
|  | jti: "def456" | expires: now + 4min                         |   |
|  | jti: "ghi789" | expires: now + 3min                         |   |
|  | (auto-prune entries older than window)                      |   |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 4: Sender Binding Verification                              |
|  Emotion: Critical -- "the core proof-of-possession check"         |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Compute the JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) of the public key           |
|  embedded in the DPoP proof header.                                |
|                                                                    |
|  Compare:                                                          |
|    computed_thumbprint  ==  ${cnf_jkt} from access token           |
|                                                                    |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|  |  DPoP Proof JWK                Access Token cnf.jkt         |   |
|  |  (public key in proof)         (thumbprint in token)        |   |
|  |                                                             |   |
|  |  thumbprint(proof.jwk) ----?=---- token.cnf.jkt             |   |
|  |                                                             |   |
|  |  MATCH    -> presenter IS the entity the token was          |   |
|  |              issued to (same key pair)                      |   |
|  |  MISMATCH -> presenter is NOT the token owner               |   |
|  |              (stolen/intercepted token) -> 401              |   |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 5: Action Scope Verification (RAR)                          |
|  Emotion: Precise -- "does this token cover this exact action?"    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  Extract the requested action from the API route + request body:   |
|    requested_action = {                                            |
|      provider: "stripe",                                           |
|      action: "invoices.create",                                    |
|      params: { customer: "cus_acme_corp", amount: 240000, ... }    |
|    }                                                               |
|                                                                    |
|  Match against ${authorization_details} from the access token:     |
|                                                                    |
|  5a. Type match: authorization_details[].type == "brain_action"    |
|  5b. Provider match: authorization_details[].provider == "stripe"  |
|  5c. Action match: authorization_details[].action ==               |
|                     "invoices.create"                               |
|  5d. Params match: requested params are within the bounds of       |
|                    authorized params                               |
|      - amount <= authorized amount (if constrained by human)       |
|      - customer matches authorized customer                        |
|                                                                    |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|  | Scope Matching Rules:                                       |   |
|  |                                                             |   |
|  | EXACT: provider + action must match exactly                 |   |
|  | PARAMS: requested params must be subset of/within           |   |
|  |         authorized params                                   |   |
|  | AMOUNT: if authorized amount is a cap, requested amount     |   |
|  |         must be <= cap                                      |   |
|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
|                                                                    |
|  Mismatch -> 403 "authorization_details_mismatch"                  |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 6: Identity and Workspace Resolution                        |
|  Emotion: Methodical -- "resolve who is acting and where"          |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  From the validated access token claims:                           |
|    1. Resolve identity from sub claim                              |
|       (existing flow from authenticateMcpRequest)                  |
|    2. Verify workspace membership                                  |
|       (existing flow using member_of relation)                     |
|    3. Extract agent_type from token claims                         |
|                                                                    |
|  This step reuses the existing identity resolution pipeline        |
|  from mcp/auth.ts with no changes.                                 |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
                               |
                               v
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  STEP 7: Action Execution                                         |
|  Emotion: Assured -- "all checks passed, safe to proceed"          |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  All verification steps passed:                                    |
|    [x] Access token valid (signature, expiry, claims)              |
|    [x] DPoP proof valid (structure, signature, freshness)          |
|    [x] Sender binding verified (thumbprint match)                  |
|    [x] Replay protection checked (jti unique)                      |
|    [x] Action scope verified (RAR authorization_details match)     |
|    [x] Identity and workspace resolved                             |
|                                                                    |
|  Execute the requested action with the verified context:           |
|    - Forward to Stripe API (or other provider)                     |
|    - Log the action with intent_id for audit trail                 |
|    - Update intent status: executing -> completed (or failed)      |
|                                                                    |
|  Response: 201 Created                                             |
|  { invoice_id: "inv_abc123", status: "open", ... }                 |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

## Error Paths

### E1: Missing DPoP Header
```
Request has Authorization: DPoP <token> but no DPoP header
-> 401 { error: "missing_dpop_proof",
         detail: "DPoP-bound token requires DPoP proof header" }
```

### E2: Bearer Scheme with DPoP-Bound Token
```
Request has Authorization: Bearer <dpop-bound-token>
-> Resource server detects cnf.jkt in token but scheme is Bearer
-> 401 { error: "invalid_token_scheme",
         detail: "Token is DPoP-bound but presented as Bearer.
                  Use 'Authorization: DPoP <token>' with DPoP proof header" }
```

### E3: Clock Skew Rejection
```
DPoP proof iat is 90 seconds in the past (window is 60 seconds)
-> 401 { error: "dpop_proof_expired",
         detail: "DPoP proof iat is outside acceptable window.
                  Ensure agent clock is synchronized (NTP)" }
```

### E4: Replay Attack Detected
```
DPoP proof jti "abc123" has already been seen in the nonce cache
-> 401 { error: "dpop_proof_reused",
         detail: "DPoP proof nonce has already been used.
                  Each request must use a unique jti" }
```

### E5: Thumbprint Mismatch (Stolen Token)
```
DPoP proof signed by key with thumbprint "thumb-BBB"
Access token cnf.jkt is "thumb-AAA"
-> 401 { error: "dpop_binding_mismatch",
         detail: "DPoP proof key does not match token binding.
                  Token may have been intercepted" }
-> Security event logged with both thumbprints for forensic analysis
```

### E6: Action Scope Mismatch
```
Token authorization_details: provider=stripe, action=invoices.create
Request: DELETE /api/integrations/stripe/invoices/inv_123
-> 403 { error: "authorization_details_mismatch",
         detail: "Token authorizes 'stripe.invoices.create'
                  but request is 'stripe.invoices.delete'" }
```

### E7: Parameter Constraint Violation
```
Token authorization_details: amount <= 200000 (constrained by human)
Request body: amount = 240000
-> 403 { error: "authorization_params_exceeded",
         detail: "Requested amount 240000 exceeds authorized cap 200000" }
```

## Backward Compatibility: Bearer Token Path

```
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|  TRANSITION PERIOD: Accept both Bearer and DPoP                   |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
|                                                                    |
|  IF Authorization header is "Bearer <token>":                      |
|    1. Check if token has cnf.jkt claim                             |
|       - YES -> reject (DPoP-bound token sent as Bearer)            |
|       - NO  -> process via existing authenticateMcpRequest         |
|    2. Traditional scope-based authorization (graph:read, etc.)     |
|    3. No RAR verification (traditional scopes only)                |
|                                                                    |
|  IF Authorization header is "DPoP <token>":                        |
|    1. Full DPoP + RAR verification pipeline (Steps 1-7 above)     |
|                                                                    |
|  Migration path:                                                   |
|    Phase 1: Both accepted (transition period)                      |
|    Phase 2: Consequential actions require DPoP                     |
|    Phase 3: All agent actions require DPoP (Bearer deprecated)     |
|                                                                    |
+------------------------------------------------------------------+
```

input.content:
journey:
  name: "Resource Server Verification"
  goal: "A resource server verifies DPoP proof-of-possession and RAR action scope before executing a requested action"
  personas:
    - id: agent-kira
      type: agent
      description: "code_agent 'Kira' presenting a DPoP-bound access token"
    - id: resource-server
      type: system
      description: "Brain API endpoint or integration proxy verifying the request"

  emotional_arc:
    start: "Vigilant -- every request is potentially hostile"
    middle: "Methodical -- each check has a clear pass/fail"
    end: "Assured -- all checks passed, request is legitimate"

  triggers:
    - "Resource server receives an HTTP request with Authorization: DPoP header"
    - "Resource server receives an HTTP request with a Bearer token containing cnf.jkt claim"

steps:
  - id: 1
    name: "Header Extraction"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Parse Authorization and DPoP headers from incoming request"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: access_token_raw
        source: "Authorization header (after 'DPoP ' prefix)"
        displayed_as: "${access_token_raw}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 2: JWT validation input"
      - name: dpop_proof_raw
        source: "DPoP header value"
        displayed_as: "${dpop_proof_raw}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 3: DPoP proof validation input"
      - name: request_method
        source: "HTTP request method"
        displayed_as: "${request_method}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 3: htm claim verification"
      - name: request_uri
        source: "HTTP request URI (scheme + host + path, no query)"
        displayed_as: "${request_uri}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 3: htu claim verification"

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Vigilant -- parse before trusting"
      exit: "Proceeding -- raw inputs extracted for validation"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      Authorization scheme MUST be "DPoP" (not "Bearer") for DPoP-bound tokens
      DPoP header MUST be present when Authorization scheme is "DPoP"
      Both headers extracted atomically -- if either is missing, fail-fast with 401

  - id: 2
    name: "Access Token Validation"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Standard JWT signature and claims validation, extended with DPoP-specific claims"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: cnf_jkt
        source: "Access token JWT cnf.jkt claim"
        displayed_as: "${cnf_jkt}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 4: Thumbprint comparison target"
      - name: authorization_details
        source: "Access token JWT authorization_details claim"
        displayed_as: "${authorization_details}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 5: Action scope matching"
      - name: token_sub
        source: "Access token JWT sub claim"
        displayed_as: "${token_sub}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 6: Identity resolution"
      - name: token_workspace
        source: "Access token JWT urn:brain:workspace claim"
        displayed_as: "${token_workspace}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 6: Workspace membership verification"
      - name: intent_id
        source: "Access token JWT urn:brain:intent_id claim"
        displayed_as: "${intent_id}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 7: Audit trail linking"

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Methodical -- standard JWT verification (existing capability)"
      exit: "Proceeding -- token is structurally valid, claims extracted"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      Reuses existing createJwtValidator from mcp/token-validation.ts
      Additional required claims for DPoP tokens: cnf.jkt, authorization_details
      Token issuer and audience must match expected values

  - id: 3
    name: "DPoP Proof Validation"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Validate DPoP proof JWT structure, signature, freshness, and uniqueness"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: proof_jwk
        source: "DPoP proof JWT header jwk field"
        displayed_as: "${proof_jwk}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 4: Thumbprint computation input"
      - name: proof_jti
        source: "DPoP proof JWT payload jti claim"
        displayed_as: "${proof_jti}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 3: Replay protection (nonce cache lookup/insert)"

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Vigilant -- this is the sender-constraining check"
      exit: "Proceeding -- proof is structurally valid and fresh"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      DPoP proof typ MUST be "dpop+jwt"
      DPoP proof alg MUST be an approved asymmetric algorithm (ES256)
      htm MUST match ${request_method}
      htu MUST match ${request_uri}
      iat MUST be within acceptable clock skew window (default: 60 seconds past, 5 seconds future)
      jti MUST NOT exist in nonce cache (replay protection)
      After validation, jti is added to nonce cache with TTL matching clock skew window

  - id: 4
    name: "Sender Binding Verification"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Verify that DPoP proof was signed by the key the token was issued to"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: computed_thumbprint
        source: "RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint of ${proof_jwk}"
        displayed_as: "${computed_thumbprint}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 4: Comparison with ${cnf_jkt}"

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Critical -- this is THE proof-of-possession check"
      exit: "Assured (if match) or Alarmed (if mismatch -- possible token theft)"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      computed_thumbprint MUST equal ${cnf_jkt} from the access token
      Mismatch indicates the token was presented by an entity other than the one it was issued to
      Mismatch MUST be logged as a security event with both thumbprints

  - id: 5
    name: "Action Scope Verification"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Verify that the token's RAR authorization_details cover the requested action"

    shared_artifacts:
      - name: requested_action
        source: "Derived from API route + request body"
        displayed_as: "${requested_action}"
        consumers:
          - "Step 5: Compared against ${authorization_details}"

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Precise -- matching specific action against specific authorization"
      exit: "Confirmed -- token covers exactly this action"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      Provider MUST match exactly
      Action MUST match exactly
      Params: requested values MUST be within authorized bounds
      Amount params: requested amount MUST be <= authorized amount (if constrained)
      If authorization_details is an array, at least one entry must match

  - id: 6
    name: "Identity and Workspace Resolution"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Resolve identity from token sub claim and verify workspace membership"

    shared_artifacts: []

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Methodical -- reusing existing identity resolution pipeline"
      exit: "Resolved -- identity and workspace context established"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      Reuses existing identity resolution from mcp/auth.ts
      Identity must exist and be a member of the claimed workspace
      Agent type extracted from token claims

  - id: 7
    name: "Action Execution"
    actor: resource-server
    description: "Execute the authorized action and update intent status"

    shared_artifacts: []

    emotional_state:
      entry: "Assured -- all verification steps passed"
      exit: "Complete -- action executed, audit trail recorded"

    integration_checkpoint: |
      Action executed with verified context (identity, workspace, authorization_details)
      Intent status updated: executing -> completed (or failed)
      Audit log entry links action to ${intent_id}

integration_validation:
  shared_artifact_consistency:
    - artifact: cnf_jkt
      must_match_across: [2, 4]
      failure_message: "cnf.jkt extracted from access token must be compared against computed thumbprint of DPoP proof JWK"

    - artifact: authorization_details
      must_match_across: [2, 5]
      failure_message: "authorization_details from access token must be used for action scope verification"

    - artifact: request_method
      must_match_across: [1, 3]
      failure_message: "HTTP method extracted in Step 1 must match htm claim in DPoP proof"

    - artifact: request_uri
      must_match_across: [1, 3]
      failure_message: "HTTP URI extracted in Step 1 must match htu claim in DPoP proof"

    - artifact: proof_jwk
      must_match_across: [3, 4]
      failure_message: "JWK from DPoP proof header must be the input for thumbprint computation in Step 4"

error_paths:
  - id: E1
    name: "Missing DPoP Header"
    trigger: "Authorization scheme is DPoP but no DPoP header present"
    step: 1
    recovery: "Client must include DPoP proof header with every DPoP-bound request"

  - id: E2
    name: "Bearer Scheme with DPoP-Bound Token"
    trigger: "Authorization: Bearer used with a token containing cnf.jkt"
    step: 1
    recovery: "Client must use 'Authorization: DPoP <token>' scheme"

  - id: E3
    name: "Clock Skew Rejection"
    trigger: "DPoP proof iat outside acceptable window"
    step: 3
    recovery: "Agent must synchronize clock (NTP) and retry"

  - id: E4
    name: "Replay Attack"
    trigger: "DPoP proof jti already in nonce cache"
    step: 3
    recovery: "Each request must use a unique jti; generate fresh UUID per request"

  - id: E5
    name: "Thumbprint Mismatch"
    trigger: "Computed thumbprint of proof JWK does not match cnf.jkt"
    step: 4
    recovery: "Token was likely intercepted; agent must re-obtain token with correct key"

  - id: E6
    name: "Action Scope Mismatch"
    trigger: "Requested action does not match any entry in authorization_details"
    step: 5
    recovery: "Agent must obtain a token scoped to the specific action it wants to perform"

  - id: E7
    name: "Parameter Constraint Violation"
    trigger: "Request params exceed authorized bounds (e.g., amount > cap)"
    step: 5
    recovery: "Agent must request within authorized bounds or obtain a new authorization"

input.content:
Feature: Resource Server DPoP and RAR Verification
  As a resource server receiving requests with DPoP-bound tokens,
  I need to verify proof-of-possession and action scope
  so that stolen tokens cannot be replayed and tokens cannot be used beyond their authorized scope.

  Background:
    Given the resource server has access to the authorization server's JWKS
    And the resource server maintains a time-windowed nonce cache for DPoP replay protection
    And the acceptable clock skew window is 60 seconds past and 5 seconds future

  # --- Job 3: Happy Path (full DPoP + RAR verification) ---

  Scenario: Valid DPoP-bound request passes all verification steps
    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token with:
      | claim                    | value                                       |
      | sub                      | identity:kira-agent-001                     |
      | cnf.jkt                  | REDACTED |
      | urn:brain:workspace      | lusaka-ws-001                               |
      | urn:brain:intent_id      | abc123                                      |
      | authorization_details[0] | type: brain_action, provider: stripe, action: invoices.create, params: { customer: cus_acme_corp, amount: 240000 } |
    And "Kira" constructs a fresh DPoP proof JWT with:
      | field | value                                          |
      | typ   | dpop+jwt                                       |
      | alg   | ES256                                          |
      | jwk   | Kira's public key (thumbprint: NzbLsXh8uD...)  |
      | htm   | POST                                           |
      | htu   | https://brain.example/api/integrations/stripe/invoices |
      | iat   | current timestamp                              |
      | jti   | unique-nonce-001                               |
    When "Kira" sends POST /api/integrations/stripe/invoices with:
      | header        | value                     |
      | Authorization | DPoP <access_token>       |
      | DPoP          | <dpop_proof>              |
    Then the resource server validates the access token signature via JWKS
    And the resource server validates the DPoP proof structure and signature
    And the resource server confirms htm "POST" matches the request method
    And the resource server confirms htu matches the request URI
    And the resource server confirms iat is within the clock skew window
    And the resource server confirms jti "unique-nonce-001" is not in the nonce cache
    And the resource server computes the DPoP proof JWK thumbprint as "NzbLsXh8uD..."
    And the computed thumbprint matches the access token cnf.jkt
    And the resource server matches the requested action against authorization_details
    And the action is executed successfully
    And the intent status is updated to "completed"

  # --- Job 3: Replay attack detected ---

  Scenario: DPoP proof reuse is rejected as replay attack
    Given agent "Kira" holds a valid DPoP-bound access token
    And "Kira" constructs a DPoP proof with jti "nonce-replay-test"
    And the resource server has already seen jti "nonce-replay-test" in the nonce cache
    When "Kira" sends the request with the reused DPoP proof
    Then the resource server rejects with 401
    And the error is "dpop_proof_reused"
    And the detail states "DPoP proof nonce has already been used"

  # --- Job 3: Stolen token detection ---

  Scenario: Stolen token presented with wrong DPoP key is rejected
    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token with cnf.jkt "thumb-AAA"
    And an attacker "Eve" has intercepted the access token
    And "Eve" constructs a DPoP proof signed with her own key (thumbprint "thumb-BBB")
    When "Eve" sends the request with Kira's token and Eve's DPoP proof
    Then the resource server computes the DPoP proof JWK thumbprint as "thumb-BBB"
    And "thumb-BBB" does not match the access token cnf.jkt "thumb-AAA"
    And the resource server rejects with 401
    And the error is "dpop_binding_mismatch"
    And a security event is logged with both thumbprints for forensic analysis

  # --- Job 3: Action scope mismatch ---

  Scenario: Token for invoice creation cannot be used for invoice deletion
    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token with authorization_details:
      | provider | action          |
      | stripe   | invoices.create |
    And "Kira" constructs a valid DPoP proof for DELETE /api/integrations/stripe/invoices/inv_123
    When "Kira" sends DELETE /api/integrations/stripe/invoices/inv_123
    Then the resource server extracts the requested action as "stripe.invoices.delete"
    And the resource server finds no matching entry in authorization_details
    And the resource server rejects with 403
    And the error is "authorization_details_mismatch"
    And the detail states the token authorizes "stripe.invoices.create" not "stripe.invoices.delete"

  # --- Job 2: Human-constrained parameter enforcement ---

  Scenario: Request exceeding human-constrained amount cap is rejected
    Given Marcus Santos constrained Kira's invoice authorization to max amount $2,000
    And the access token authorization_details includes params.amount cap 200000
    And "Kira" constructs a valid DPoP proof
    When "Kira" sends POST with request body amount 240000 ($2,400)
    Then the resource server compares requested amount 240000 against authorized cap 200000
    And the resource server rejects with 403
    And the error is "authorization_params_exceeded"
    And the detail states "Requested amount 240000 exceeds authorized cap 200000"

  # --- Error: Missing DPoP header ---

  Scenario: DPoP-bound token presented without DPoP proof header
    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token (contains cnf.jkt)
    When "Kira" sends the request with "Authorization: DPoP <token>" but no DPoP header
    Then the resource server rejects with 401
    And the error is "missing_dpop_proof"
    And the detail states "DPoP-bound token requires DPoP proof header"

  # --- Error: Wrong authorization scheme ---

  Scenario: DPoP-bound token presented as Bearer is rejected
    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token (contains cnf.jkt)
    When "Kira" sends the request with "Authorization: Bearer <token>" instead of "DPoP"
    Then the resource server detects cnf.jkt in the token but scheme is Bearer
    And the resource server rejects with 401
    And the error is "invalid_token_scheme"
    And the detail states "Token is DPoP-bound but presented as Bearer"

  # --- Error: Clock skew ---

  Scenario: DPoP proof with excessive clock skew is rejected
    Given agent "Kira" constructs a DPoP proof with iat 90 seconds in the past
    And the acceptable window is 60 seconds past
    When "Kira" sends the request with the stale DPoP proof
    Then the resource server rejects with 401
    And the error is "dpop_proof_expired"
    And the detail suggests clock synchronization via NTP

  # --- Habit path: Bearer tokens still work for non-consequential actions ---

  Scenario: Traditional Bearer token accepted for non-consequential action
    Given agent "Kira" holds a traditional Bearer token with scope "graph:read"
    And the token does NOT contain a cnf.jkt claim
    When "Kira" sends GET /api/workspaces/lusaka-ws-001/entities with "Authorization: Bearer <token>"
    Then the resource server processes via existing authenticateMcpRequest pipeline
    And no DPoP verification is required
    And the request is authorized via traditional scope validation

  # --- Property: Sender binding is unforgeable ---

  @property
  Scenario: Only the holder of the original private key can present a DPoP-bound token
    Given a DPoP-bound access token with cnf.jkt bound to a specific ES256 key pair
    When any entity without the matching private key attempts to construct a valid DPoP proof
    Then the DPoP proof signature verification fails
    And the request is rejected regardless of access token validity

  # --- Property: Each DPoP proof is single-use ---

  @property
  Scenario: No DPoP proof jti can be accepted twice within the nonce window
    Given the nonce cache retains jti values for the duration of the clock skew window
    When a DPoP proof with a previously-seen jti is presented
    Then the request is rejected with "dpop_proof_reused"
    And no action is executed

  # --- Property: Authorization details are immutable post-issuance ---

  @property
  Scenario: Access token authorization_details cannot be modified after issuance
    Given a DPoP-bound access token with specific authorization_details
    When any entity attempts to modify the authorization_details
    Then the access token JWT signature verification fails
    And the request is rejected as invalid token

input.content:
# Shared Artifacts Registry: OAuth RAR + DPoP

## Purpose

This registry tracks every data value that flows across multiple steps in the two journeys (Agent Token Acquisition, Resource Server Verification). Each artifact has a single source of truth and documented consumers. Untracked artifacts are the primary cause of horizontal integration failures.

---

## Cross-Journey Artifacts

These artifacts flow across BOTH journeys — they are produced in Journey 1 and consumed in Journey 2.

### action_spec / authorization_details

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Agent task context (Journey 1, Step 1) |
| Type | `ActionSpec = { provider: string, action: string, params?: Record<string, unknown> }` |
| Owner | Intent system (`app/src/server/intent/types.ts`) |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — this is the central authorization object flowing through both journeys |
| Consumers | J1-Step 1: constructed by agent |
| | J1-Step 3: submitted as intent `action_spec` |
| | J1-Step 4: displayed to human in consent notification |
| | J1-Step 5: sent as `authorization_details` in token request |
| | J1-Step 6: embedded in access token JWT claims |
| | J2-Step 2: extracted from access token claims |
| | J2-Step 5: matched against requested action |
| Validation | `action_spec` in intent MUST be structurally identical to `authorization_details` in token request and token claims. If human constrains via Step 4, the constrained version becomes the new source of truth. |
| Transformation | `ActionSpec` -> `authorization_details[]`: wrapped in array with `type: "brain_action"` prefix |

### jwk_thumbprint / cnf.jkt

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Computed from agent's DPoP public key via RFC 7638 (Journey 1, Step 2) |
| Type | `string` (Base64url-encoded SHA-256 hash of canonical JWK) |
| Owner | Agent runtime key store |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — mismatch means token is unusable or indicates theft |
| Consumers | J1-Step 2: computed from generated key pair |
| | J1-Step 3: submitted as `dpop_jwk_thumbprint` in intent |
| | J1-Step 5: verified against DPoP proof key in token request |
| | J1-Step 6: embedded as `cnf.jkt` in access token |
| | J2-Step 2: extracted from access token as `cnf_jkt` |
| | J2-Step 4: compared against computed thumbprint of proof JWK |
| Validation | Must be deterministic: same key always produces same thumbprint. Must match across intent submission, token issuance, and every resource server verification. |

### intent_id

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Brain API response from POST /api/intents (Journey 1, Step 3) |
| Type | `string` (SurrealDB record ID, e.g., "abc123") |
| Owner | Intent system |
| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** — links token to authorizing intent for audit trail |
| Consumers | J1-Step 3: returned in intent creation response |
| | J1-Step 4: displayed in human consent notification |
| | J1-Step 5: sent as `intent_id` parameter in token request |
| | J1-Step 6: embedded as `urn:brain:intent_id` in access token |
| | J2-Step 2: extracted from access token claims |
| | J2-Step 7: linked in audit log entry |
| Validation | Must reference a valid intent record with status "authorized" at time of token issuance |

### access_token

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Authorization server token endpoint response (Journey 1, Step 6) |
| Type | Signed JWT string |
| Owner | Authorization server |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — the bearer credential for the entire resource server interaction |
| Consumers | J1-Step 6: issued by authorization server |
| | J1-Step 7: sent in Authorization header to resource server |
| | J2-Step 1: extracted from Authorization header |
| | J2-Step 2: validated (signature, expiry, claims) |
| Validation | Must be a valid JWT signed by the authorization server's current signing key. Must contain cnf.jkt, authorization_details, sub, urn:brain:workspace, urn:brain:intent_id claims. |

---

## Journey 1-Only Artifacts

### dpop_private_key

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Agent runtime key store, in-memory (Journey 1, Step 2) |
| Type | ES256 private key (CryptoKey or JWK) |
| Owner | Agent sandbox runtime |
| Integration Risk | **CRITICAL** — if this leaks, DPoP is defeated |
| Consumers | J1-Step 2: generated and stored |
| | J1-Step 5: used to sign DPoP proof for token request |
| | J1-Step 7: used to sign DPoP proof for resource server |
| Validation | Must NEVER leave the agent sandbox. Must NEVER be persisted to disk or transmitted over network. Destroyed when sandbox terminates. |

### risk_score

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | LLM Evaluator output via `evaluateIntent` pipeline (Journey 1, Step 3) |
| Type | `number` (0-100) |
| Owner | Intent authorizer (`app/src/server/intent/authorizer.ts`) |
| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** — determines routing path |
| Consumers | J1-Step 3: routing decision (auto_approve / veto_window / reject) |
| | J1-Step 4: displayed to human in consent notification |
| Validation | Must be within [0, 100]. Threshold for auto-approve is 30 (configurable). |

### routing_decision

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | `routeByRisk` function output (Journey 1, Step 3) |
| Type | `RoutingDecision = { route: "auto_approve" } | { route: "veto_window", expires_at: Date } | { route: "reject", reason: string }` |
| Owner | Risk router (`app/src/server/intent/risk-router.ts`) |
| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** — determines which path the journey takes |
| Consumers | J1-Step 3: determines path (auto_approve -> Step 5, veto_window -> Step 4, reject -> error) |
| | J1-Step 4: veto window expiry time |
| Validation | Must be one of the three discriminated union variants |

### consent_decision

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Human action via consent UI (Journey 1, Step 4) |
| Type | `"approve" | "constrain" | "veto"` |
| Owner | Human operator |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — determines whether token can be issued |
| Consumers | J1-Step 4: recorded in intent/vetoed_by relation |
| | J1-Step 5: determines whether token request proceeds |
| Validation | Must be one of the three values. Veto must include a reason. Constrain must include modified params. |

### constrained_params

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Human modification via consent UI [Constrain...] action (Journey 1, Step 4) |
| Type | Partial modification of `authorization_details` params |
| Owner | Human operator |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — modifies the authorization boundary |
| Consumers | J1-Step 5: modified `authorization_details` in token request |
| | J1-Step 6: modified `authorization_details` in token claims |
| | J2-Step 5: resource server enforces constrained bounds |
| Validation | Must produce valid `authorization_details`. Constraints must be more restrictive than original (cannot expand scope). |

---

## Journey 2-Only Artifacts

### dpop_proof

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Signed by agent's DPoP private key per-request (Journey 2 input) |
| Type | JWT string with typ "dpop+jwt" |
| Owner | Agent runtime |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — the proof-of-possession artifact |
| Consumers | J2-Step 1: extracted from DPoP header |
| | J2-Step 3: validated (structure, signature, freshness, uniqueness) |
| | J2-Step 4: JWK extracted for thumbprint computation |
| Validation | Must be a fresh JWT for each request. htm must match request method. htu must match request URI. jti must be unique within the nonce window. |

### proof_jti (nonce)

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | DPoP proof JWT payload jti claim |
| Type | `string` (UUID or random identifier) |
| Owner | Agent runtime (generates), resource server nonce cache (tracks) |
| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** — replay protection depends on this |
| Consumers | J2-Step 3: checked against nonce cache, then stored |
| Validation | Must be unique per request. Nonce cache entries auto-expire after clock skew window. |

### computed_thumbprint

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Computed at resource server from DPoP proof JWK (Journey 2, Step 4) |
| Type | `string` (Base64url-encoded SHA-256 hash) |
| Owner | Resource server (computed, not stored) |
| Integration Risk | **HIGH** — this is the sender binding verification result |
| Consumers | J2-Step 4: compared against cnf_jkt from access token |
| Validation | Must equal cnf_jkt for the request to be authorized. Computation must follow RFC 7638. |

### requested_action

| Property | Value |
|---|---|
| Source of Truth | Derived from API route + request body at resource server (Journey 2, Step 5) |
| Type | `{ provider: string, action: string, params: Record<string, unknown> }` |
| Owner | Resource server route handler |
| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** — must be derived consistently from the HTTP request |
| Consumers | J2-Step 5: compared against authorization_details from access token |
| Validation | Derivation rules must be deterministic: same HTTP request always produces same requested_action. |

---

## Integration Checkpoints Summary

| Checkpoint | Artifacts | Failure Mode |
|---|---|---|
| Intent-to-token sync | action_spec, authorization_details | Token request rejected if authorization_details does not match intent action_spec |
| Key binding chain | jwk_thumbprint, cnf.jkt, computed_thumbprint | Token request rejected (Step 5) or resource request rejected (Step 4) |
| Intent traceability | intent_id | Token cannot be issued without valid authorized intent |
| DPoP freshness | proof_jti, nonce cache | Replayed proofs rejected at resource server |
| Action scope | authorization_details, requested_action | Resource server rejects out-of-scope actions |
| Constraint propagation | constrained_params, authorization_details | Human constraints must flow from consent -> token -> resource server |

input.content:
# Requirements: OAuth RAR + DPoP

## Business Context

The Brain platform orchestrates AI agents that perform consequential actions on behalf of human operators — creating invoices, deploying services, managing resources. The existing authorization system has two disconnected layers: the intent system (structured `action_spec` with LLM evaluation and risk routing) and the OAuth system (coarse-grained scopes like `task:write`). This feature bridges them using RFC 9396 (Rich Authorization Requests) and RFC 9449 (DPoP), creating a unified authorization flow where structured intents become OAuth authorization objects and tokens are cryptographically bound to the presenting agent.

## Business Rules

### BR-1: Action Tiering
- Consequential actions (external API calls, financial operations, deployments) require RAR authorization with structured `authorization_details`
- Non-consequential actions (graph reads, observation writes, entity searches) continue using traditional OAuth scopes
- The existing risk router threshold (default: risk_score <= 30) determines the auto-approve boundary

### BR-2: Intent-Token Binding
- Every RAR access token MUST reference a valid, authorized intent via `urn:brain:intent_id` claim
- The token's `authorization_details` MUST match the intent's `action_spec` (or its human-constrained variant)
- No RAR token can be issued without an authorized intent — the intent system remains the authorization decision point

### BR-3: Sender Constraining
- DPoP-bound tokens are cryptographically tied to the agent's key pair via `cnf.jkt` claim
- The agent's DPoP public key thumbprint is registered with the intent at submission time
- Only the holder of the matching private key can present the token to a resource server
- Private keys MUST NOT leave the agent sandbox boundary

### BR-4: Human Oversight
- Actions entering `veto_window` (risk_score > 30, decision = APPROVE) present RAR `authorization_details` to the human operator in human-readable form
- Humans can approve, constrain (reduce scope/budget), or veto
- Constraints modify the `authorization_details` that flow into the issued token
- Veto window expiry (default: 30 minutes) auto-authorizes medium-risk actions unless explicitly vetoed

### BR-5: Replay Protection
- Each DPoP proof MUST contain a unique `jti` nonce
- Resource servers maintain a time-windowed nonce cache (entries auto-expire after clock skew window)
- Reused `jti` values are rejected with a specific error code
- The nonce cache MUST be scoped per resource server instance (injected via dependency injection, not module-level singleton)

### BR-6: Backward Compatibility
- Existing Bearer token authentication MUST continue working for non-consequential actions during the transition period
- Bearer tokens with `cnf.jkt` claims (DPoP-bound) presented via `Authorization: Bearer` scheme MUST be rejected with an actionable error message
- Migration path: Phase 1 (both accepted) -> Phase 2 (consequential actions require DPoP) -> Phase 3 (all agent actions require DPoP)

## Functional Requirements

### FR-1: DPoP Key Pair Management
- Agent runtimes generate ES256 key pairs within their sandbox
- Key pairs persist for the duration of the agent session (not per-action)
- JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) computed from the public key is the binding identifier
- Key pair lifecycle: generated at session start, destroyed at sandbox termination

### FR-2: Intent Submission with DPoP Binding
- Extend the existing intent submission endpoint to accept `dpop_jwk_thumbprint`
- The thumbprint links the intent to the agent's key pair before token issuance
- The existing 4-stage evaluation pipeline (Policy Gate -> LLM Evaluator -> Risk Router -> Status Update) is preserved unchanged

### FR-3: RAR Token Endpoint
- New grant type: `urn:brain:intent-authorization`
- Accepts `intent_id` and `authorization_details` parameters
- Validates DPoP proof header (structure, signature, freshness)
- Verifies intent exists, is authorized, and requestor identity matches
- Verifies authorization_details matches intent action_spec
- Verifies DPoP proof key thumbprint matches intent dpop_jwk_thumbprint
- Issues DPoP-bound access token with `cnf.jkt`, `authorization_details`, `urn:brain:intent_id` claims
- Short TTL for consequential action tokens (default: 300 seconds)

### FR-4: RAR Consent Rendering
- Transform `authorization_details` JSON into human-readable consent display
- Provider-specific rendering rules (e.g., Stripe amounts in dollars, not cents)
- Display risk score, LLM evaluator reasoning, and veto window expiry
- Support approve, constrain, and veto actions
- Constrain action produces modified authorization_details with tighter bounds

### FR-5: DPoP Proof Verification at Resource Server
- Extract and validate DPoP proof from request headers
- Verify proof structure (typ, alg, jwk), signature, and claims (htm, htu, iat, jti)
- Compute JWK thumbprint and compare against access token `cnf.jkt`
- Check `jti` against nonce cache for replay protection
- Clock skew tolerance: configurable, default 60 seconds past, 5 seconds future

### FR-6: RAR Action Scope Verification at Resource Server
- Extract requested action from API route and request body
- Match against access token `authorization_details`
- Provider and action must match exactly
- Request parameters must be within authorized bounds (e.g., amount <= authorized cap)

### FR-7: Audit Trail
- Every intent submission, evaluation, routing decision, human consent action, token issuance, and resource server verification is logged
- Audit entries link to intent_id and include DPoP JWK thumbprint
- Security events (thumbprint mismatch, replay detection) are logged at elevated severity

## Non-Functional Requirements

### NFR-1: Performance
- DPoP key pair generation: < 50ms per session
- DPoP proof construction: < 5ms per request
- Token endpoint response time: < 200ms (excluding intent evaluation, which is async)
- DPoP proof verification at resource server: < 10ms per request
- Nonce cache lookup: < 1ms

### NFR-2: Security
- Private keys never leave the agent sandbox boundary (enforced by runtime isolation, not application code)
- DPoP proofs are single-use (replay protection via nonce cache)
- Access tokens for consequential actions have short TTL (300 seconds default)
- Clock skew tolerance is configurable but defaults to conservative values
- All verification failures produce specific, actionable error codes (not generic 401)

### NFR-3: Reliability
- LLM evaluator timeout (30 seconds) falls back to APPROVE with risk_score=50, routing to veto_window (existing behavior preserved)
- Nonce cache entries auto-expire (no unbounded memory growth)
- Token re-issuance is allowed within intent expiry window if original token expires before use

### NFR-4: Compatibility
- TypeScript/Bun runtime
- SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL mode for all new tables
- Functional paradigm: pure functions, pipeline composition, no classes
- No `null` in domain data — use `undefined` via optional properties
- No module-level mutable singletons — nonce cache and key stores injected via dependency injection
- Existing Better Auth IdP integration preserved — new functionality layers on top

## Dependencies

| Dependency | Status | Impact |
|---|---|---|
| Better Auth IdP (existing) | Available | Token endpoint extension, JWKS integration |
| Intent Node (existing) | Available | action_spec structure, evaluation pipeline, risk router |
| Unified Identity (existing) | Available | Identity resolution for token claims |
| Authority System (existing) | Available | Permission matrix for intent lifecycle actions |
| jose library (existing) | Available | JWT signing/verification, JWKS, JWK thumbprint |
| SurrealDB (existing) | Available | Schema extension for DPoP binding fields |

## Risks

| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
|---|---|---|---|
| Clock skew between agent sandboxes and resource server | Medium | Medium | Configurable tolerance window, NTP requirement for agent runtimes, actionable error messages |
| Nonce cache memory growth under high throughput | Low | Medium | Time-windowed set with automatic expiry, dependency-injected per instance |
| DPoP complexity discouraging agent developers | Medium | High | Client library abstracts key management and proof construction |
| Human consent fatigue from too many RAR requests | Medium | High | Action tiering (BR-1) ensures only consequential actions trigger consent |
| Better Auth plugin compatibility with custom grant type | Low | High | Spike to validate custom grant type registration before implementation |
