   155→    Then the Custom AS rejects with 401 "dpop_key_mismatch"
   156→    And the error detail states "DPoP proof signed by different key than registered with intent"
   157→
   158→  # --- Error: Token expired before use ---
   159→
   160→  Scenario: Actor re-requests token after expiry
   161→    Given agent "Kira" obtained a DPoP-bound token with 300-second TTL
   162→    And the token expired before "Kira" could execute the operation
   163→    When "Kira" requests a new token for the same authorized intent
   164→    Then the Custom AS issues a new DPoP-bound token
   165→    And the new token has a fresh 300-second TTL
   166→    And the new DPoP proof uses a fresh jti nonce
   167→
   168→  # --- Error: Authorizer Agent timeout ---
   169→
   170→  Scenario: Authorizer Agent timeout falls back to veto window
   171→    Given agent "Kira" submits an intent for a Brain operation
   172→    When the Authorizer Agent times out after 30 seconds
   173→    Then the system falls back to APPROVE with risk_score 50 and policy_only true
   174→    And the risk router selects route "veto_window" (score 50 > threshold 30)
   175→    And the intent enters "pending_veto" for human review
   176→    And the evaluation reason states "Authorizer Agent timeout -- falling back to policy-only with veto window"
   177→
   178→  # --- Property: Token binding invariant ---
   179→
   180→  @property
   181→  Scenario: DPoP-bound tokens cannot be used without the matching private key
   182→    Given a DPoP-bound access token has been issued with cnf.jkt "thumb-AAA"
   183→    When any entity presents the token without a valid DPoP proof signed by key "thumb-AAA"
   184→    Then the Brain resource server rejects the request with 401
   185→    And no operation is executed regardless of token validity
   186→
   187→  # --- Property: Uniform authorization ---
   188→
   189→  @property
   190→  Scenario: Every Brain operation requires brain_action authorization_details
   191→    Given any actor (agent or human) attempts a Brain operation
   192→    When the actor presents a token without authorization_details containing a brain_action
   193→    Then the Brain resource server rejects the request
   194→    And no scope-based fallback is attempted
   195→    And no Bearer token path is available
   196→
   197→  # --- Property: Human parity ---
   198→
   199→  @property
   200→  Scenario: Human and agent tokens are verified identically at Brain boundary
   201→    Given a DPoP-bound token issued to agent "Kira" for a brain_action
   202→    And a DPoP-bound token issued to human "Marcus" for the same brain_action
   203→    When both tokens are presented to the Brain resource server with valid DPoP proofs
   204→    Then the same verification pipeline runs for both tokens
   205→    And no distinction is made between human and agent tokens at the Brain boundary
   206→

1→# Journey: Brain Resource Server Verification (Sovereign Hybrid Model)
     2→
     3→## Overview
     4→
     5→The complete flow when the Brain resource server receives ANY request -- from an agent sandbox or a human dashboard client -- bearing a DPoP-bound access token. The Brain has ONE verification pipeline: DPoP proof-of-possession + RAR brain_action scope. No scope fallback. No Bearer path. No distinction between human and agent at the Brain boundary.
     6→
     7→## Actors
     8→
     9→- **Any Actor** (agent "Kira" or human "Marcus" via Bridge -- the token presenter)
    10→- **Brain Resource Server** (the single verification pipeline for ALL Brain operations)
    11→- **Nonce Cache** (time-windowed set for DPoP replay protection)
    12→
    13→## Emotional Arc
    14→
    15→```
    16→Start: Vigilant          Middle: Methodical           End: Assured
    17→  |                          |                           |
    18→  v                          v                           v
    19→"Every request is       "Each check has a          "All checks passed --
    20→ potentially hostile"    clear pass/fail"           request is legitimate"
    21→```
    22→
    23→## Verification Pipeline
    24→
    25→```
    26→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    27→|  INCOMING REQUEST (from ANY actor -- agent or human)              |
    28→|                                                                    |
    29→|  POST /api/brain/integrations/stripe/invoices                      |
    30→|  Authorization: DPoP eyJhbGciOiJFUzI1NiIs...  (access token)      |
    31→|  DPoP: eyJ0eXAiOiJkcG9wK2p3dCIs...            (proof JWT)         |
    32→|  Content-Type: application/json                                    |
    33→|  { customer: "cus_acme_corp", amount: 240000, currency: "usd" }   |
    34→|                                                                    |
    35→|  NOTE: No "Authorization: Bearer" path exists to the Brain.        |
    36→|  NOTE: No session cookie path exists to the Brain.                 |
    37→|  NOTE: No scope-based authorization exists at the Brain.           |
    38→|                                                                    |
    39→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
    40→                               |
    41→                               v
    42→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    43→|  STEP 1: Header Extraction                                        |
    44→|  Emotion: Vigilant -- "parse before trusting"                      |
    45→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    46→|                                                                    |
    47→|  Extract from request:                                             |
    48→|    1. Authorization header -> token_type + access_token            |
    49→|       - MUST be "DPoP <token>"                                     |
    50→|       - "Bearer <token>" -> 401 "dpop_required"                    |
    51→|       - Session cookie only -> 401 "dpop_required"                 |
    52→|       - Missing header -> 401 "dpop_required"                      |
    53→|    2. DPoP header -> dpop_proof (JWT)                              |
    54→|       - MUST be present                                            |
    55→|       - Missing DPoP header -> 401 "missing_dpop_proof"            |
    56→|                                                                    |
    57→|  Fail-fast: missing or malformed headers -> 401 immediately        |
    58→|  No fallback to scopes. No fallback to Bearer. One path only.      |
    59→|                                                                    |
    60→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
    61→                               |
    62→                               v
    63→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    64→|  STEP 2: Access Token Validation                                  |
    65→|  Emotion: Methodical -- "standard JWT verification"                |
    66→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    67→|                                                                    |
    68→|  Validate the access token JWT:                                    |
    69→|    1. Signature verification via JWKS (Custom AS signing key)      |
    70→|    2. Issuer: "https://brain.example/api/auth"                     |
    71→|    3. Audience: "https://brain.example"                            |
    72→|    4. Expiration: exp > now (with clock tolerance)                 |
    73→|    5. Required claims present: sub, cnf.jkt,                       |
    74→|       authorization_details, urn:brain:workspace                   |
    75→|                                                                    |
    76→|  Extract for downstream checks:                                    |
    77→|    ${cnf_jkt} = token.cnf.jkt                                      |
    78→|    ${authorization_details} = token.authorization_details           |
    79→|    ${workspace_id} = token["urn:brain:workspace"]                   |
    80→|    ${intent_id} = token["urn:brain:intent_id"]                     |
    81→|                                                                    |
    82→|  Tokens MUST contain authorization_details with type               |
    83→|  "brain_action". Tokens without authorization_details are          |
    84→|  rejected -- no scope fallback.                                    |
    85→|                                                                    |
    86→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
    87→                               |
    88→                               v
    89→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    90→|  STEP 3: DPoP Proof Validation                                    |
    91→|  Emotion: Vigilant -- "this is the sender-constraining check"      |
    92→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    93→|                                                                    |
    94→|  Validate the DPoP proof JWT:                                      |
    95→|                                                                    |
    96→|  3a. Structure check:                                              |
    97→|    - Header typ MUST be "dpop+jwt"                                 |
    98→|    - Header alg MUST be an approved asymmetric algorithm (ES256)   |
    99→|    - Header jwk MUST contain the public key                        |
   100→|                                                                    |
   101→|  3b. Signature verification:                                       |
   102→|    - Verify proof signature using the embedded JWK public key      |
   103→|                                                                    |
   104→|  3c. Claims validation:                                            |
   105→|    - htm (HTTP method) MUST match the request method ("POST")      |
   106→|    - htu (HTTP URI) MUST match the request URI                     |
   107→|    - iat (issued at) MUST be within acceptable window              |
   108→|      (e.g., now - 60s <= iat <= now + 5s for clock skew)           |
   109→|    - jti (unique identifier) MUST be present                       |
   110→|                                                                    |
   111→|  3d. Replay protection:                                            |
   112→|    - Check jti against the nonce cache                             |
   113→|    - If jti already seen -> 401 "dpop_proof_reused"                |
   114→|    - If jti is fresh -> add to nonce cache with TTL                |
   115→|                                                                    |
   116→|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
   117→|  | Nonce Cache (time-windowed set)                             |   |
   118→|  |------------------------------------------------------------|   |
   119→|  | jti: "abc123" | expires: now + 5min                         |   |
   120→|  | jti: "def456" | expires: now + 4min                         |   |
   121→|  | jti: "ghi789" | expires: now + 3min                         |   |
   122→|  | (auto-prune entries older than window)                      |   |
   123→|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
   124→|                                                                    |
   125→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
   126→                               |
   127→                               v
   128→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   129→|  STEP 4: Sender Binding Verification                              |
   130→|  Emotion: Critical -- "the core proof-of-possession check"         |
   131→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   132→|                                                                    |
   133→|  Compute the JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) of the public key           |
   134→|  embedded in the DPoP proof header.                                |
   135→|                                                                    |
   136→|  Compare:                                                          |
   137→|    computed_thumbprint  ==  ${cnf_jkt} from access token           |
   138→|                                                                    |
   139→|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
   140→|  |  DPoP Proof JWK                Access Token cnf.jkt         |   |
   141→|  |  (public key in proof)         (thumbprint in token)        |   |
   142→|  |                                                             |   |
   143→|  |  thumbprint(proof.jwk) ----?=---- token.cnf.jkt             |   |
   144→|  |                                                             |   |
   145→|  |  MATCH    -> presenter IS the entity the token was          |   |
   146→|  |              issued to (same key pair)                      |   |
   147→|  |  MISMATCH -> presenter is NOT the token owner               |   |
   148→|  |              (stolen/intercepted token) -> 401              |   |
   149→|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
   150→|                                                                    |
   151→|  This check is identical for agents and humans. The Brain          |
   152→|  does not know or care whether the presenter is human or agent.    |
   153→|                                                                    |
   154→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
   155→                               |
   156→                               v
   157→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   158→|  STEP 5: Operation Scope Verification (RAR brain_action)          |
   159→|  Emotion: Precise -- "does this token cover this exact operation?" |
   160→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   161→|                                                                    |
   162→|  Extract the requested operation from the API route + body:        |
   163→|    requested_action = {                                            |
   164→|      type: "brain_action",                                         |
   165→|      action: "create",                                             |
   166→|      resource: "invoice",                                          |
   167→|      constraints: {                                                |
   168→|        provider: "stripe",                                         |
   169→|        customer: "cus_acme_corp",                                  |
   170→|        amount: 240000                                              |
   171→|      }                                                             |
   172→|    }                                                               |
   173→|                                                                    |
   174→|  Match against ${authorization_details} from the access token:     |
   175→|                                                                    |
   176→|  5a. Type match: authorization_details[].type == "brain_action"    |
   177→|  5b. Action match: authorization_details[].action == "create"      |
   178→|  5c. Resource match: authorization_details[].resource == "invoice" |
   179→|  5d. Constraints match: requested constraints are within the       |
   180→|      bounds of authorized constraints                              |
   181→|      - amount <= authorized amount (if constrained by human)       |
   182→|      - provider matches authorized provider                        |
   183→|                                                                    |
   184→|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
   185→|  | Scope Matching Rules:                                       |   |
   186→|  |                                                             |   |
   187→|  | TYPE: must be "brain_action" (always)                       |   |
   188→|  | ACTION: must match exactly (read, create, update, delete)   |   |
   189→|  | RESOURCE: must match exactly (knowledge_graph, invoice, ...) |   |
   190→|  | CONSTRAINTS: requested values must be within authorized      |   |
   191→|  |              bounds                                         |   |
   192→|  +------------------------------------------------------------+   |
   193→|                                                                    |
   194→|  Mismatch -> 403 "authorization_details_mismatch"                  |
   195→|                                                                    |
   196→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
   197→                               |
   198→                               v
   199→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   200→|  STEP 6: Identity and Workspace Resolution                        |
   201→|  Emotion: Methodical -- "resolve who is acting and where"          |
   202→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   203→|                                                                    |
   204→|  From the validated access token claims:                           |
   205→|    1. Resolve identity from sub claim                              |
   206→|       - Could be "identity:kira-agent-001" (agent)                 |
   207→|       - Could be "identity:marcus-human-001" (human via Bridge)    |
   208→|    2. Verify workspace membership                                  |
   209→|       (existing flow using member_of relation)                     |
   210→|    3. Extract actor_type from identity record                      |
   211→|                                                                    |
   212→|  The Brain does not differentiate verification logic based on      |
   213→|  actor type. All actors are equal at the Brain boundary.           |
   214→|                                                                    |
   215→+------------------------------|-------------------------------------+
   216→                               |
   217→                               v
   218→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   219→|  STEP 7: Operation Execution                                      |
   220→|  Emotion: Assured -- "all checks passed, safe to proceed"          |
   221→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   222→|                                                                    |
   223→|  All verification steps passed:                                    |
   224→|    [x] Access token valid (signature, expiry, claims)              |
   225→|    [x] authorization_details contains brain_action (not scopes)    |
   226→|    [x] DPoP proof valid (structure, signature, freshness)          |
   227→|    [x] Sender binding verified (thumbprint match)                  |
   228→|    [x] Replay protection checked (jti unique)                      |
   229→|    [x] Operation scope verified (brain_action match)               |
   230→|    [x] Identity and workspace resolved                             |
   231→|                                                                    |
   232→|  Execute the requested operation with the verified context:        |
   233→|    - Forward to integration (Stripe, deployment, etc.)             |
   234→|    - Or execute Brain graph operation directly                     |
   235→|    - Log the operation with intent_id for audit trail              |
   236→|    - Update intent status: executing -> completed (or failed)      |
   237→|                                                                    |
   238→|  Response: 201 Created                                             |
   239→|  { invoice_id: "inv_abc123", status: "open", ... }                 |
   240→|                                                                    |
   241→+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   242→```
   243→
   244→## Error Paths
   245→
   246→### E1: Missing Authorization Header / Session Cookie Only
   247→```
   248→Request has no Authorization header (or only a session cookie)
   249→-> 401 { error: "dpop_required",
   250→         detail: "Brain operations require DPoP-bound RAR tokens.
   251→                  Session cookies cannot access the Brain.
   252→                  Use the Bridge to exchange your session for a Brain token." }
   253→```
   254→
   255→### E2: Bearer Scheme Rejected
   256→```
   257→Request has Authorization: Bearer <any-token>
   258→-> 401 { error: "dpop_required",
   259→         detail: "Brain does not accept Bearer tokens.
   260→                  All operations require DPoP-bound tokens with
   261→                  brain_action authorization_details." }
   262→No distinction between DPoP-bound tokens sent as Bearer vs plain Bearer.
   263→The Brain only speaks DPoP.
   264→```
   265→
   266→### E3: Missing DPoP Proof Header
   267→```
   268→Request has Authorization: DPoP <token> but no DPoP header
   269→-> 401 { error: "missing_dpop_proof",
   270→         detail: "DPoP-bound token requires DPoP proof header" }
   271→```
   272→
   273→### E4: Clock Skew Rejection
   274→```
   275→DPoP proof iat is 90 seconds in the past (window is 60 seconds)
   276→-> 401 { error: "dpop_proof_expired",
   277→         detail: "DPoP proof iat is outside acceptable window.
   278→                  Ensure client clock is synchronized (NTP)" }
   279→```
   280→
   281→### E5: Replay Attack Detected
   282→```
   283→DPoP proof jti "abc123" has already been seen in the nonce cache
   284→-> 401 { error: "dpop_proof_reused",
   285→         detail: "DPoP proof nonce has already been used.
   286→                  Each request must use a unique jti" }
   287→```
   288→
   289→### E6: Thumbprint Mismatch (Stolen Token)
   290→```
   291→DPoP proof signed by key with thumbprint "thumb-BBB"
   292→Access token cnf.jkt is "thumb-AAA"
   293→-> 401 { error: "dpop_binding_mismatch",
   294→         detail: "DPoP proof key does not match token binding.
   295→                  Token may have been intercepted" }
   296→-> Security event logged with both thumbprints for forensic analysis
   297→```
   298→
   299→### E7: Operation Scope Mismatch
   300→```
   301→Token authorization_details: action=create, resource=invoice
   302→Request: DELETE /api/brain/integrations/stripe/invoices/inv_123
   303→-> 403 { error: "authorization_details_mismatch",
   304→         detail: "Token authorizes 'create invoice'
   305→                  but request is 'delete invoice'" }
   306→```
   307→
   308→### E8: Constraint Violation
   309→```
   310→Token authorization_details: constraints.amount <= 200000 (constrained by human)
   311→Request body: amount = 240000
   312→-> 403 { error: "authorization_params_exceeded",
   313→         detail: "Requested amount 240000 exceeds authorized cap 200000" }
   314→```
   315→
   316→### E9: Missing authorization_details
   317→```
   318→Token does not contain authorization_details claim
   319→(e.g., a legacy token from before the Sovereign Hybrid Model)
   320→-> 401 { error: "missing_authorization_details",
   321→         detail: "Brain tokens must contain brain_action
   322→                  authorization_details. No scope fallback." }
   323→```
   324→

1→journey:
     2→  name: "Brain Resource Server Verification (Sovereign Hybrid Model)"
     3→  goal: "The Brain resource server verifies DPoP proof-of-possession and RAR brain_action scope for EVERY request -- no scope fallback, no Bearer path, no actor type distinction"
     4→  personas:
     5→    - id: any-actor
     6→      type: any
     7→      description: "Agent or human presenting a DPoP-bound access token (indistinguishable at Brain boundary)"
     8→    - id: brain-resource-server
     9→      type: system
    10→      description: "Brain API -- single verification pipeline for ALL operations"
    11→
    12→  emotional_arc:
    13→    start: "Vigilant -- every request is potentially hostile"
    14→    middle: "Methodical -- each check has a clear pass/fail"
    15→    end: "Assured -- all checks passed, request is legitimate"
    16→
    17→  triggers:
    18→    - "Brain resource server receives ANY HTTP request (from agent sandbox or human dashboard via Bridge)"
    19→
    20→  architectural_principles:
    21→    - "ONE verification pipeline -- no branching on Bearer vs DPoP, no scope vs RAR paths"
    22→    - "Brain boundary rejects session cookies, Bearer tokens, and scope-only tokens"
    23→    - "Human and agent tokens are verified identically"
    24→    - "authorization_details must contain type: brain_action -- always"
    25→
    26→steps:
    27→  - id: 1
    28→    name: "Header Extraction"
    29→    actor: brain-resource-server
    30→    description: "Parse Authorization and DPoP headers -- reject anything that is not DPoP"
    31→
    32→    shared_artifacts:
    33→      - name: access_token_raw
    34→        source: "Authorization header (after 'DPoP ' prefix)"
    35→        displayed_as: "${access_token_raw}"
    36→        consumers:
    37→          - "Step 2: JWT validation input"
    38→      - name: dpop_proof_raw
    39→        source: "DPoP header value"
    40→        displayed_as: "${dpop_proof_raw}"
    41→        consumers:
    42→          - "Step 3: DPoP proof validation input"
    43→      - name: request_method
    44→        source: "HTTP request method"
    45→        displayed_as: "${request_method}"
    46→        consumers:
    47→          - "Step 3: htm claim verification"
    48→      - name: request_uri
    49→        source: "HTTP request URI (scheme + host + path, no query)"
    50→        displayed_as: "${request_uri}"
    51→        consumers:
    52→          - "Step 3: htu claim verification"
    53→
    54→    emotional_state:
    55→      entry: "Vigilant -- parse before trusting"
    56→      exit: "Proceeding -- raw inputs extracted for validation"
    57→
    58→    integration_checkpoint: |
    59→      Authorization scheme MUST be "DPoP" -- reject Bearer, session cookies, and missing headers
    60→      DPoP header MUST be present when Authorization scheme is "DPoP"
    61→      Both headers extracted atomically -- if either is missing, fail-fast with 401
    62→      No fallback. No scope path. Brain speaks DPoP+RAR only.
    63→
    64→  - id: 2
    65→    name: "Access Token Validation"
    66→    actor: brain-resource-server
    67→    description: "JWT validation with mandatory brain_action authorization_details"
    68→
    69→    shared_artifacts:
    70→      - name: cnf_jkt
    71→        source: "Access token JWT cnf.jkt claim"
    72→        displayed_as: "${cnf_jkt}"
    73→        consumers:
    74→          - "Step 4: Thumbprint comparison target"
    75→      - name: authorization_details
    76→        source: "Access token JWT authorization_details claim"
    77→        displayed_as: "${authorization_details}"
    78→        consumers:
    79→          - "Step 5: Operation scope matching"
    80→      - name: token_sub
    81→        source: "Access token JWT sub claim"
    82→        displayed_as: "${token_sub}"
    83→        consumers:
    84→          - "Step 6: Identity resolution"
    85→      - name: token_workspace
    86→        source: "Access token JWT urn:brain:workspace claim"
    87→        displayed_as: "${token_workspace}"
    88→        consumers:
    89→          - "Step 6: Workspace membership verification"
    90→      - name: intent_id
    91→        source: "Access token JWT urn:brain:intent_id claim"
    92→        displayed_as: "${intent_id}"
    93→        consumers:
    94→          - "Step 7: Audit trail linking"
    95→
    96→    emotional_state:
    97→      entry: "Methodical -- JWT verification extended with brain_action requirements"
    98→      exit: "Proceeding -- token is structurally valid, claims extracted"
    99→
   100→    integration_checkpoint: |
   101→      Token MUST contain authorization_details with at least one entry of type "brain_action"
   102→      Tokens without authorization_details are rejected -- no scope fallback
   103→      Token issuer must be the Custom AS (not Better Auth)
   104→      Token audience must match the Brain resource server
   105→
   106→  - id: 3
   107→    name: "DPoP Proof Validation"
   108→    actor: brain-resource-server
   109→    description: "Validate DPoP proof JWT structure, signature, freshness, and uniqueness"
   110→
   111→    shared_artifacts:
   112→      - name: proof_jwk
   113→        source: "DPoP proof JWT header jwk field"
   114→        displayed_as: "${proof_jwk}"
   115→        consumers:
   116→          - "Step 4: Thumbprint computation input"
   117→      - name: proof_jti
   118→        source: "DPoP proof JWT payload jti claim"
   119→        displayed_as: "${proof_jti}"
   120→        consumers:
   121→          - "Step 3: Replay protection (nonce cache lookup/insert)"
   122→
   123→    emotional_state:
   124→      entry: "Vigilant -- this is the sender-constraining check"
   125→      exit: "Proceeding -- proof is structurally valid and fresh"
   126→
   127→    integration_checkpoint: |
   128→      DPoP proof typ MUST be "dpop+jwt"
   129→      DPoP proof alg MUST be an approved asymmetric algorithm (ES256)
   130→      htm MUST match ${request_method}
   131→      htu MUST match ${request_uri}
   132→      iat MUST be within acceptable clock skew window (default: 60 seconds past, 5 seconds future)
   133→      jti MUST NOT exist in nonce cache (replay protection)
   134→      After validation, jti is added to nonce cache with TTL matching clock skew window
   135→
   136→  - id: 4
   137→    name: "Sender Binding Verification"
   138→    actor: brain-resource-server
   139→    description: "Verify that DPoP proof was signed by the key the token was issued to"
   140→
   141→    shared_artifacts:
   142→      - name: computed_thumbprint
   143→        source: "RFC 7638 JWK thumbprint of ${proof_jwk}"
   144→        displayed_as: "${computed_thumbprint}"
   145→        consumers:
   146→          - "Step 4: Comparison with ${cnf_jkt}"
   147→
   148→    emotional_state:
   149→      entry: "Critical -- this is THE proof-of-possession check"
   150→      exit: "Assured (if match) or Alarmed (if mismatch -- possible token theft)"
   151→
   152→    integration_checkpoint: |
   153→      computed_thumbprint MUST equal ${cnf_jkt} from the access token
   154→      Mismatch indicates the token was presented by an entity other than the one it was issued to
   155→      Mismatch MUST be logged as a security event with both thumbprints
   156→      This check is identical for agent and human tokens
   157→
   158→  - id: 5
   159→    name: "Operation Scope Verification (brain_action)"
   160→    actor: brain-resource-server
   161→    description: "Verify that the token's brain_action authorization_details cover the requested operation"
   162→
   163→    shared_artifacts:
   164→      - name: requested_action
   165→        source: "Derived from API route + request body"
   166→        displayed_as: "${requested_action}"
   167→        consumers:
   168→          - "Step 5: Compared against ${authorization_details}"
   169→
   170→    emotional_state:
   171→      entry: "Precise -- matching specific operation against specific authorization"
   172→      exit: "Confirmed -- token covers exactly this operation"
   173→
   174→    integration_checkpoint: |
   175→      type MUST be "brain_action" (always)
   176→      action MUST match exactly (read, create, update, delete)
   177→      resource MUST match exactly (knowledge_graph, invoice, task, ...)
   178→      constraints: requested values MUST be within authorized bounds
   179→      If authorization_details is an array, at least one entry must match
   180→
   181→  - id: 6
   182→    name: "Identity and Workspace Resolution"
   183→    actor: brain-resource-server
   184→    description: "Resolve identity from token sub claim -- could be agent or human"
   185→
   186→    shared_artifacts: []
   187→
   188→    emotional_state:
   189→      entry: "Methodical -- identity resolution does not distinguish actor type"
   190→      exit: "Resolved -- identity and workspace context established"
   191→
   192→    integration_checkpoint: |
   193→      Identity must exist and be a member of the claimed workspace
   194→      Identity could be agent (identity:kira-agent-001) or human (identity:marcus-human-001)
   195→      Brain does not differentiate verification logic based on actor type
   196→
   197→  - id: 7
   198→    name: "Operation Execution"
   199→    actor: brain-resource-server
   200→    description: "Execute the authorized operation and update intent status"
   201→
   202→    shared_artifacts: []
   203→
   204→    emotional_state:
   205→      entry: "Assured -- all verification steps passed"
   206→      exit: "Complete -- operation executed, audit trail recorded"
   207→
   208→    integration_checkpoint: |
   209→      Operation executed with verified context (identity, workspace, authorization_details)
   210→      Intent status updated: executing -> completed (or failed)
   211→      Audit log entry links operation to ${intent_id}
   212→      Audit format is identical for agent and human operations
   213→
   214→integration_validation:
   215→  shared_artifact_consistency:
   216→    - artifact: cnf_jkt
   217→      must_match_across: [2, 4]
   218→      failure_message: "cnf.jkt extracted from access token must be compared against computed thumbprint of DPoP proof JWK"
   219→
   220→    - artifact: authorization_details
   221→      must_match_across: [2, 5]
   222→      failure_message: "authorization_details from access token must be used for operation scope verification"
   223→
   224→    - artifact: request_method
   225→      must_match_across: [1, 3]
   226→      failure_message: "HTTP method extracted in Step 1 must match htm claim in DPoP proof"
   227→
   228→    - artifact: request_uri
   229→      must_match_across: [1, 3]
   230→      failure_message: "HTTP URI extracted in Step 1 must match htu claim in DPoP proof"
   231→
   232→    - artifact: proof_jwk
   233→      must_match_across: [3, 4]
   234→      failure_message: "JWK from DPoP proof header must be the input for thumbprint computation in Step 4"
   235→
   236→error_paths:
   237→  - id: E1
   238→    name: "No DPoP Authorization (Bearer/Cookie/Missing)"
   239→    trigger: "Request does not have Authorization: DPoP scheme"
   240→    step: 1
   241→    recovery: "Client must use DPoP-bound RAR tokens. Use Bridge for session exchange."
   242→
   243→  - id: E2
   244→    name: "Missing DPoP Proof Header"
   245→    trigger: "Authorization scheme is DPoP but no DPoP header present"
   246→    step: 1
   247→    recovery: "Client must include DPoP proof header with every request"
   248→
   249→  - id: E3
   250→    name: "Clock Skew Rejection"
   251→    trigger: "DPoP proof iat outside acceptable window"
   252→    step: 3
   253→    recovery: "Actor must synchronize clock (NTP) and retry"
   254→
   255→  - id: E4
   256→    name: "Replay Attack"
   257→    trigger: "DPoP proof jti already in nonce cache"
   258→    step: 3
   259→    recovery: "Each request must use a unique jti; generate fresh UUID per request"
   260→
   261→  - id: E5
   262→    name: "Thumbprint Mismatch"
   263→    trigger: "Computed thumbprint of proof JWK does not match cnf.jkt"
   264→    step: 4
   265→    recovery: "Token was likely intercepted; actor must re-obtain token with correct key"
   266→
   267→  - id: E6
   268→    name: "Operation Scope Mismatch"
   269→    trigger: "Requested operation does not match any brain_action entry in authorization_details"
   270→    step: 5
   271→    recovery: "Actor must obtain a token scoped to the specific operation it wants to perform"
   272→
   273→  - id: E7
   274→    name: "Constraint Violation"
   275→    trigger: "Request params exceed authorized bounds (e.g., amount > cap)"
   276→    step: 5
   277→    recovery: "Actor must request within authorized bounds or obtain a new authorization"
   278→
   279→  - id: E8
   280→    name: "Missing authorization_details"
   281→    trigger: "Token does not contain authorization_details with brain_action type"
   282→    step: 2
   283→    recovery: "Token must be obtained from Custom AS with brain_action authorization_details. No scope fallback."
   284→

1→Feature: Brain Resource Server DPoP and RAR Verification (Sovereign Hybrid Model)
     2→  As the Brain resource server receiving ALL requests,
     3→  I need to verify DPoP proof-of-possession and brain_action authorization_details
     4→  so that every Brain operation is uniformly authorized -- no scope fallback, no Bearer path,
     5→  and human/agent tokens are verified identically.
     6→
     7→  Background:
     8→    Given the Brain resource server has access to the Custom AS JWKS
     9→    And the Brain resource server maintains a time-windowed nonce cache for DPoP replay protection
    10→    And the acceptable clock skew window is 60 seconds past and 5 seconds future
    11→    And the Brain does NOT accept Bearer tokens, session cookies, or scope-only tokens
    12→    And the Brain verifies agent and human tokens with the same pipeline
    13→
    14→  # --- Happy Path: Agent request with full DPoP + RAR verification ---
    15→
    16→  Scenario: Valid agent DPoP-bound request passes all verification steps
    17→    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token with:
    18→      | claim                    | value                                       |
    19→      | sub                      | identity:kira-agent-001                     |
    20→      | cnf.jkt                  | REDACTED |
    21→      | urn:brain:workspace      | lusaka-ws-001                               |
    22→      | urn:brain:intent_id      | abc123                                      |
    23→      | authorization_details[0] | type: brain_action, action: create, resource: invoice, constraints: { provider: stripe, customer: cus_acme_corp, amount: 240000 } |
    24→    And "Kira" constructs a fresh DPoP proof JWT with:
    25→      | field | value                                          |
    26→      | typ   | dpop+jwt                                       |
    27→      | alg   | ES256                                          |
    28→      | jwk   | Kira's public key (thumbprint: NzbLsXh8uD...)  |
    29→      | htm   | POST                                           |
    30→      | htu   | https://brain.example/api/brain/integrations/stripe/invoices |
    31→      | iat   | current timestamp                              |
    32→      | jti   | unique-nonce-001                               |
    33→    When "Kira" sends POST /api/brain/integrations/stripe/invoices with:
    34→      | header        | value                     |
    35→      | Authorization | DPoP <access_token>       |
    36→      | DPoP          | <dpop_proof>              |
    37→    Then the Brain validates the access token signature via JWKS
    38→    And the Brain confirms authorization_details contains type "brain_action"
    39→    And the Brain validates the DPoP proof structure and signature
    40→    And the Brain confirms htm "POST" matches the request method
    41→    And the Brain confirms htu matches the request URI
    42→    And the Brain confirms iat is within the clock skew window
    43→    And the Brain confirms jti "unique-nonce-001" is not in the nonce cache
    44→    And the Brain computes the DPoP proof JWK thumbprint as "NzbLsXh8uD..."
    45→    And the computed thumbprint matches the access token cnf.jkt
    46→    And the Brain matches the requested operation against brain_action authorization_details
    47→    And the operation is executed successfully
    48→    And the intent status is updated to "completed"
    49→
    50→  # --- Happy Path: Human request via Bridge (identical verification) ---
    51→
    52→  Scenario: Valid human DPoP-bound request via Bridge passes same verification
    53→    Given human "Marcus" obtained a DPoP-bound token via the Bridge with:
    54→      | claim                    | value                                       |
    55→      | sub                      | identity:marcus-human-001                   |
    56→      | cnf.jkt                  | BrowserKey-abc123-thumbprint                |
    57→      | urn:brain:workspace      | lusaka-ws-001                               |
    58→      | authorization_details[0] | type: brain_action, action: read, resource: knowledge_graph, constraints: { project: lusaka, depth: 2 } |
    59→    And Marcus's dashboard constructs a fresh DPoP proof signed with the browser key pair
    60→    When the dashboard sends GET /api/brain/projects/lusaka/graph with:
    61→      | header        | value                     |
    62→      | Authorization | DPoP <access_token>       |
    63→      | DPoP          | <dpop_proof>              |
    64→    Then the Brain runs the SAME verification pipeline as for agent requests
    65→    And the Brain validates brain_action authorization_details
    66→    And the Brain verifies DPoP proof and sender binding
    67→    And the request is processed successfully
    68→
    69→  # --- Replay attack detected ---
    70→
    71→  Scenario: DPoP proof reuse is rejected as replay attack
    72→    Given any actor holds a valid DPoP-bound access token
    73→    And the actor constructs a DPoP proof with jti "nonce-replay-test"
    74→    And the Brain has already seen jti "nonce-replay-test" in the nonce cache
    75→    When the actor sends the request with the reused DPoP proof
    76→    Then the Brain rejects with 401
    77→    And the error is "dpop_proof_reused"
    78→    And the detail states "DPoP proof nonce has already been used"
    79→
    80→  # --- Stolen token detection ---
    81→
    82→  Scenario: Stolen token presented with wrong DPoP key is rejected
    83→    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token with cnf.jkt "thumb-AAA"
    84→    And an attacker "Eve" has intercepted the access token
    85→    And "Eve" constructs a DPoP proof signed with her own key (thumbprint "thumb-BBB")
    86→    When "Eve" sends the request with Kira's token and Eve's DPoP proof
    87→    Then the Brain computes the DPoP proof JWK thumbprint as "thumb-BBB"
    88→    And "thumb-BBB" does not match the access token cnf.jkt "thumb-AAA"
    89→    And the Brain rejects with 401
    90→    And the error is "dpop_binding_mismatch"
    91→    And a security event is logged with both thumbprints for forensic analysis
    92→
    93→  # --- Operation scope mismatch ---
    94→
    95→  Scenario: Token for invoice creation cannot be used for invoice deletion
    96→    Given agent "Kira" holds a DPoP-bound access token with authorization_details:
    97→      | type         | action | resource |
    98→      | brain_action | create | invoice  |
    99→    And "Kira" constructs a valid DPoP proof for DELETE /api/brain/integrations/stripe/invoices/inv_123
   100→    When "Kira" sends DELETE /api/brain/integrations/stripe/invoices/inv_123
   101→    Then the Brain extracts the requested operation as brain_action: delete invoice
   102→    And the Brain finds no matching entry in authorization_details
   103→    And the Brain rejects with 403
   104→    And the error is "authorization_details_mismatch"
   105→
   106→  # --- Human-constrained parameter enforcement ---
   107→
   108→  Scenario: Request exceeding human-constrained amount cap is rejected
   109→    Given Marcus Santos constrained Kira's invoice authorization to max amount $2,000
   110→    And the access token authorization_details includes constraints.amount cap 200000
   111→    And "Kira" constructs a valid DPoP proof
   112→    When "Kira" sends POST with request body amount 240000 ($2,400)
   113→    Then the Brain compares requested amount 240000 against authorized cap 200000
   114→    And the Brain rejects with 403
   115→    And the error is "authorization_params_exceeded"
   116→    And the detail states "Requested amount 240000 exceeds authorized cap 200000"
   117→
   118→  # --- Bearer token rejected at Brain boundary ---
   119→
   120→  Scenario: Bearer token rejected at Brain boundary
   121→    Given any actor holds a Bearer token (with or without cnf.jkt)
   122→    When the actor sends a request with "Authorization: Bearer <token>"
   123→    Then the Brain rejects with 401
   124→    And the error is "dpop_required"
   125→    And the detail states "Brain does not accept Bearer tokens. All operations require DPoP-bound tokens with brain_action authorization_details."
   126→
   127→  # --- Session cookie rejected at Brain boundary ---
   128→
   129→  Scenario: Session cookie rejected at Brain boundary
   130→    Given human "Marcus" has a valid Better Auth session cookie
   131→    When the dashboard sends a request to the Brain with only the session cookie (no Authorization header)
   132→    Then the Brain rejects with 401
   133→    And the error is "dpop_required"
   134→    And the detail states "Brain operations require DPoP-bound RAR tokens. Use the Bridge to exchange your session."
   135→
   136→  # --- Missing DPoP header ---
   137→
   138→  Scenario: DPoP-bound token presented without DPoP proof header
   139→    Given any actor holds a DPoP-bound access token
   140→    When the actor sends the request with "Authorization: DPoP <token>" but no DPoP header
   141→    Then the Brain rejects with 401
   142→    And the error is "missing_dpop_proof"
   143→    And the detail states "DPoP-bound token requires DPoP proof header"
   144→
   145→  # --- Clock skew ---
   146→
   147→  Scenario: DPoP proof with excessive clock skew is rejected
   148→    Given any actor constructs a DPoP proof with iat 90 seconds in the past
   149→    And the acceptable window is 60 seconds past
   150→    When the actor sends the request with the stale DPoP proof
   151→    Then the Brain rejects with 401
   152→    And the error is "dpop_proof_expired"
   153→    And the detail suggests clock synchronization via NTP
   154→
   155→  # --- Missing authorization_details ---
   156→
   157→  Scenario: Token without brain_action authorization_details is rejected
   158→    Given any actor holds a token that does not contain authorization_details
   159→    When the actor sends the request with "Authorization: DPoP <token>" and a valid DPoP proof
   160→    Then the Brain rejects with 401
   161→    And the error is "missing_authorization_details"
   162→    And the detail states "Brain tokens must contain brain_action authorization_details. No scope fallback."
   163→
   164→  # --- Property: Sender binding is unforgeable ---
   165→
   166→  @property
   167→  Scenario: Only the holder of the original private key can present a DPoP-bound token
   168→    Given a DPoP-bound access token with cnf.jkt bound to a specific ES256 key pair
   169→    When any entity without the matching private key attempts to construct a valid DPoP proof
   170→    Then the DPoP proof signature verification fails
   171→    And the request is rejected regardless of access token validity
   172→
   173→  # --- Property: Each DPoP proof is single-use ---
   174→
   175→  @property
   176→  Scenario: No DPoP proof jti can be accepted twice within the nonce window
   177→    Given the nonce cache retains jti values for the duration of the clock skew window
   178→    When a DPoP proof with a previously-seen jti is presented
   179→    Then the request is rejected with "dpop_proof_reused"
   180→    And no operation is executed
   181→
   182→  # --- Property: Brain speaks one language ---
   183→
   184→  @property
   185→  Scenario: The Brain only accepts brain_action authorization_details
   186→    Given any token presented to the Brain resource server
   187→    When the token contains scope claims but no authorization_details with type "brain_action"
   188→    Then the request is rejected
   189→    And no scope-based authorization is attempted
   190→
   191→  # --- Property: Human-agent parity ---
   192→
   193→  @property
   194→  Scenario: Human and agent tokens are indistinguishable at Brain boundary
   195→    Given a DPoP-bound token issued to an agent
   196→    And a DPoP-bound token issued to a human via the Bridge
   197→    When both tokens are presented with valid DPoP proofs for the same brain_action
   198→    Then the same verification pipeline runs for both
   199→    And the Brain does not differentiate based on actor type
   200→

1→# Shared Artifacts Registry: OAuth RAR + DPoP (Sovereign Hybrid Model)
     2→
     3→## Purpose
     4→
     5→This registry tracks every data value that flows across multiple steps in the two journeys (Actor Token Acquisition, Brain Resource Server Verification) and the Bridge path. Each artifact has a single source of truth and documented consumers. Untracked artifacts are the primary cause of horizontal integration failures.
     6→
     7→## Architectural Constraint
     8→
     9→**No `scope` artifact is tracked in this registry.** Better Auth scopes exist only at the dashboard UI authentication layer. They never flow to the Brain. The Brain speaks one language: `brain_action` authorization_details.
    10→
    11→---
    12→
    13→## Cross-Journey Artifacts
    14→
    15→These artifacts flow across BOTH journeys -- they are produced in Journey 1 (Token Acquisition) and consumed in Journey 2 (Brain Resource Server Verification).
    16→
    17→### brain_action / authorization_details
    18→
    19→| Property | Value |
    20→|---|---|
    21→| Source of Truth | Actor context -- agent task context, dashboard UI action, or CLI command (Journey 1, Step 1) |
    22→| Type | `BrainAction = { type: "brain_action", action: string, resource: string, constraints?: Record<string, unknown> }` |
    23→| Owner | Custom Authorization Server |
    24→| Integration Risk | **CRITICAL** -- this is the ONLY authorization object in the entire system. The Authorizer Agent evaluates brain_action objects, never scopes. |
    25→| Consumers | J1-Step 1: constructed by actor (agent or human dashboard) |
    26→| | J1-Step 3: submitted as `authorization_details` in intent |
    27→| | J1-Step 4: displayed to human in consent notification |
    28→| | J1-Step 5: sent as `authorization_details` in token request |
    29→| | J1-Step 6: embedded in access token JWT claims |
    30→| | J1-Bridge B2: constructed by dashboard client for Bridge exchange |
    31→| | J2-Step 2: extracted from access token claims |
    32→| | J2-Step 5: matched against requested operation |
    33→| Validation | `authorization_details` MUST contain at least one entry with `type: "brain_action"`. This is enforced at EVERY boundary: Custom AS token issuance, Brain resource server verification. No scope fallback. |
    34→| Transformation | Actor intent -> `authorization_details[]`: wrapped in array, every entry has `type: "brain_action"` |
    35→| Key Principle | **Every request to the Brain, even a simple graph read, carries a brain_action.** There is no "this operation is too simple for RAR" exception. |
    36→
    37→### jwk_thumbprint / cnf.jkt
    38→
    39→| Property | Value |
    40→|---|---|
    41→| Source of Truth | Computed from actor's DPoP public key via RFC 7638 (Journey 1, Step 2) |
    42→| Type | `string` (Base64url-encoded SHA-256 hash of canonical JWK) |
    43→| Owner | Actor runtime key store (agent sandbox, browser, or CLI process) |
    44→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- mismatch means token is unusable or indicates theft |
    45→| Consumers | J1-Step 2: computed from generated key pair |
    46→| | J1-Step 3: submitted as `dpop_jwk_thumbprint` in intent |
    47→| | J1-Step 5: verified against DPoP proof key in token request |
    48→| | J1-Step 6: embedded as `cnf.jkt` in access token |
    49→| | J1-Bridge B2: computed from browser DPoP key pair |
    50→| | J2-Step 2: extracted from access token as `cnf_jkt` |
    51→| | J2-Step 4: compared against computed thumbprint of proof JWK |
    52→| Validation | Must be deterministic: same key always produces same thumbprint. Must match across intent submission, token issuance, and every Brain resource server verification. |
    53→| Key Principle | **Both agents and humans (via Bridge) have DPoP key pairs.** Agent keys live in sandbox memory. Human keys live in browser memory. Both are ephemeral. |
    54→
    55→### intent_id
    56→
    57→| Property | Value |
    58→|---|---|
    59→| Source of Truth | Custom AS response from POST /api/auth/intents (Journey 1, Step 3) |
    60→| Type | `string` (SurrealDB record ID, e.g., "abc123") |
    61→| Owner | Custom Authorization Server |
    62→| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** -- links token to authorizing intent for audit trail |
    63→| Consumers | J1-Step 3: returned in intent creation response |
    64→| | J1-Step 4: displayed in human consent notification |
    65→| | J1-Step 5: sent as `intent_id` parameter in token request |
    66→| | J1-Step 6: embedded as `urn:brain:intent_id` in access token |
    67→| | J2-Step 2: extracted from access token claims |
    68→| | J2-Step 7: linked in audit log entry |
    69→| Validation | Must reference a valid intent record with status "authorized" at time of token issuance |
    70→
    71→### access_token
    72→
    73→| Property | Value |
    74→|---|---|
    75→| Source of Truth | Custom AS token endpoint response (Journey 1, Step 6) |
    76→| Type | Signed JWT string |
    77→| Owner | Custom Authorization Server |
    78→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- the credential for the entire Brain resource server interaction |
    79→| Consumers | J1-Step 6: issued by Custom AS |
    80→| | J1-Step 7: sent in Authorization header to Brain resource server |
    81→| | J1-Bridge B3: sent by dashboard to Brain resource server |
    82→| | J2-Step 1: extracted from Authorization header |
    83→| | J2-Step 2: validated (signature, expiry, claims) |
    84→| Validation | Must be a valid JWT signed by the Custom AS signing key. Must contain cnf.jkt, authorization_details (with brain_action type), sub, urn:brain:workspace. |
    85→| Key Principle | **Issued by the Custom AS, NOT by Better Auth.** Better Auth sessions cannot become access tokens for the Brain. The Bridge exchanges sessions for Custom AS tokens. |
    86→
    87→---
    88→
    89→## Journey 1-Only Artifacts
    90→
    91→### dpop_private_key
    92→
    93→| Property | Value |
    94→|---|---|
    95→| Source of Truth | Actor runtime key store, in-memory (Journey 1, Step 2) |
    96→| Type | ES256 private key (CryptoKey or JWK) |
    97→| Owner | Actor runtime (agent sandbox, browser, or CLI process) |
    98→| Integration Risk | **CRITICAL** -- if this leaks, DPoP is defeated |
    99→| Consumers | J1-Step 2: generated and stored |
   100→| | J1-Step 5: used to sign DPoP proof for token request |
   101→| | J1-Step 7: used to sign DPoP proof for Brain resource server |
   102→| | J1-Bridge B2: generated in browser for human Bridge exchange |
   103→| Validation | Must NEVER leave the actor's runtime boundary. Must NEVER be persisted to disk or transmitted over network. Destroyed when runtime terminates (sandbox shutdown, browser tab close, CLI exit). |
   104→
   105→### risk_score
   106→
   107→| Property | Value |
   108→|---|---|
   109→| Source of Truth | Authorizer Agent output via evaluation pipeline (Journey 1, Step 3) |
   110→| Type | `number` (0-100) |
   111→| Owner | Custom Authorization Server |
   112→| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** -- determines routing path (auto-approve vs veto window) |
   113→| Consumers | J1-Step 3: routing decision (auto_approve / veto_window / reject) |
   114→| | J1-Step 4: displayed to human in consent notification |
   115→| Validation | Must be within [0, 100]. Threshold for auto-approve is 30 (configurable). |
   116→| Key Principle | **Risk routing determines notification path, NOT authorization mechanism.** All operations use RAR regardless of risk score. Low-risk reads auto-approve; high-risk writes go to veto window. |
   117→
   118→### routing_decision
   119→
   120→| Property | Value |
   121→|---|---|
   122→| Source of Truth | `routeByRisk` function output (Journey 1, Step 3) |
   123→| Type | `RoutingDecision = { route: "auto_approve" } | { route: "veto_window", expires_at: Date } | { route: "reject", reason: string }` |
   124→| Owner | Custom Authorization Server |
   125→| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** -- determines which path the journey takes |
   126→| Consumers | J1-Step 3: determines path (auto_approve -> Step 5, veto_window -> Step 4, reject -> error) |
   127→| | J1-Step 4: veto window expiry time |
   128→| Validation | Must be one of the three discriminated union variants |
   129→
   130→### consent_decision
   131→
   132→| Property | Value |
   133→|---|---|
   134→| Source of Truth | Human action via consent UI (Journey 1, Step 4) |
   135→| Type | `"approve" | "constrain" | "veto"` |
   136→| Owner | Human operator |
   137→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- determines whether token can be issued |
   138→| Consumers | J1-Step 4: recorded in intent/vetoed_by relation |
   139→| | J1-Step 5: determines whether token request proceeds |
   140→| Validation | Must be one of the three values. Veto must include a reason. Constrain must include modified params. |
   141→
   142→### constrained_params
   143→
   144→| Property | Value |
   145→|---|---|
   146→| Source of Truth | Human modification via consent UI [Constrain...] action (Journey 1, Step 4) |
   147→| Type | Partial modification of `authorization_details` constraints |
   148→| Owner | Human operator |
   149→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- modifies the authorization boundary |
   150→| Consumers | J1-Step 5: modified `authorization_details` in token request |
   151→| | J1-Step 6: modified `authorization_details` in token claims |
   152→| | J2-Step 5: Brain resource server enforces constrained bounds |
   153→| Validation | Must produce valid `authorization_details`. Constraints must be more restrictive than original (cannot expand scope). |
   154→
   155→### better_auth_session (Bridge Only)
   156→
   157→| Property | Value |
   158→|---|---|
   159→| Source of Truth | Better Auth login response (Journey 1, Bridge B1) |
   160→| Type | Session cookie / session token |
   161→| Owner | Better Auth IdP |
   162→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- session hijacking must NOT grant Brain access |
   163→| Consumers | J1-Bridge B1: issued by Better Auth on login |
   164→| | J1-Bridge B2: sent to Custom AS for session validation |
   165→| Validation | Better Auth session = authentication proof ONLY ("I am Marcus"). It does NOT authorize any Brain operation. The Custom AS validates the session is active, then issues its own DPoP-bound RAR token. |
   166→| Key Principle | **Scopes are for the front door only.** Better Auth scopes (e.g., "dashboard:access") are NOT consumed by the Brain. They are consumed by the dashboard UI to determine what UI features to show. The Brain never sees them. |
   167→
   168→---
   169→
   170→## Journey 2-Only Artifacts
   171→
   172→### dpop_proof
   173→
   174→| Property | Value |
   175→|---|---|
   176→| Source of Truth | Signed by actor's DPoP private key per-request (Journey 2 input) |
   177→| Type | JWT string with typ "dpop+jwt" |
   178→| Owner | Actor runtime |
   179→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- the proof-of-possession artifact |
   180→| Consumers | J2-Step 1: extracted from DPoP header |
   181→| | J2-Step 3: validated (structure, signature, freshness, uniqueness) |
   182→| | J2-Step 4: JWK extracted for thumbprint computation |
   183→| Validation | Must be a fresh JWT for each request. htm must match request method. htu must match request URI. jti must be unique within the nonce window. |
   184→
   185→### proof_jti (nonce)
   186→
   187→| Property | Value |
   188→|---|---|
   189→| Source of Truth | DPoP proof JWT payload jti claim |
   190→| Type | `string` (UUID or random identifier) |
   191→| Owner | Actor runtime (generates), Brain resource server nonce cache (tracks) |
   192→| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** -- replay protection depends on this |
   193→| Consumers | J2-Step 3: checked against nonce cache, then stored |
   194→| Validation | Must be unique per request. Nonce cache entries auto-expire after clock skew window. |
   195→| Key Principle | **Nonce cache must handle ALL requests, not just consequential ones.** Since every Brain operation uses DPoP, the nonce cache must be sized for total request volume. |
   196→
   197→### computed_thumbprint
   198→
   199→| Property | Value |
   200→|---|---|
   201→| Source of Truth | Computed at Brain resource server from DPoP proof JWK (Journey 2, Step 4) |
   202→| Type | `string` (Base64url-encoded SHA-256 hash) |
   203→| Owner | Brain resource server (computed, not stored) |
   204→| Integration Risk | **HIGH** -- this is the sender binding verification result |
   205→| Consumers | J2-Step 4: compared against cnf_jkt from access token |
   206→| Validation | Must equal cnf_jkt for the request to be authorized. Computation must follow RFC 7638. |
   207→
   208→### requested_action
   209→
   210→| Property | Value |
   211→|---|---|
   212→| Source of Truth | Derived from API route + request body at Brain resource server (Journey 2, Step 5) |
   213→| Type | `{ type: "brain_action", action: string, resource: string, constraints: Record<string, unknown> }` |
   214→| Owner | Brain resource server route handler |
   215→| Integration Risk | **MEDIUM** -- must be derived consistently from the HTTP request |
   216→| Consumers | J2-Step 5: compared against authorization_details from access token |
   217→| Validation | Derivation rules must be deterministic: same HTTP request always produces same requested_action. Type must always be "brain_action". |
   218→
   219→---
   220→
   221→## Removed Artifacts (Sovereign Hybrid Model)
   222→
   223→The following artifacts from the previous tiered model have been removed:
   224→
   225→| Artifact | Reason for Removal |
   226→|---|---|
   227→| `scope` | Better Auth scopes exist only at the dashboard UI layer. They never flow to the Brain. The Brain does not evaluate scopes. |
   228→| `bearer_token` (Brain path) | The Brain does not accept Bearer tokens. All operations require DPoP-bound tokens. |
   229→| `action_tier` / `consequential_flag` | Classification is a vulnerability. There is no "consequential vs non-consequential" boundary. All operations use RAR uniformly. |
   230→
   231→---
   232→
   233→## Integration Checkpoints Summary
   234→
   235→| Checkpoint | Artifacts | Failure Mode |
   236→|---|---|---|
   237→| Intent-to-token sync | brain_action, authorization_details | Token request rejected if authorization_details does not match intent brain_action |
   238→| Key binding chain | jwk_thumbprint, cnf.jkt, computed_thumbprint | Token request rejected (Step 5) or Brain request rejected (Step 4) |
   239→| Intent traceability | intent_id | Token cannot be issued without valid authorized intent |
   240→| DPoP freshness | proof_jti, nonce cache | Replayed proofs rejected at Brain resource server |
   241→| Operation scope | authorization_details, requested_action | Brain rejects out-of-scope operations |
   242→| Constraint propagation | constrained_params, authorization_details | Human constraints must flow from consent -> token -> Brain resource server |
   243→| Session-to-token separation | better_auth_session, access_token | Session cookie CANNOT access Brain directly. Must exchange via Bridge. |
   244→| Uniform format | authorization_details | Agent and human tokens use identical brain_action format. Brain does not distinguish actor type. |
   245→

1→# Requirements: OAuth RAR + DPoP (Sovereign Hybrid Model)
     2→
     3→## Business Context
     4→
     5→The Brain platform orchestrates AI agents and human operators who perform operations on a shared knowledge graph (SurrealDB). The existing authorization system conflates authentication with authorization: Better Auth sessions grant direct Brain access, and agents use coarse-grained scopes (`task:write`) that cannot distinguish "read a project summary" from "delete all project data." The Sovereign Hybrid Model replaces this with two cleanly separated layers:
     6→
     7→1. **Better Auth (Human Front Door)**: Standard sessions/scopes for human login to the dashboard UI. Scopes mean "I am a logged-in human." This is the entry point and NOTHING more.
     8→2. **Custom Authorization Server (Brain Operations)**: Full RAR + Full DPoP for ANY actor (human or agent) performing ANY operation on the knowledge graph. No scopes. No tiering. Every operation is a structured `brain_action` intent.
     9→3. **The Bridge**: When a human needs to touch the Brain, they exchange their Better Auth session for a DPoP-bound RAR token from the Custom AS.
    10→
    11→## Business Rules
    12→
    13→### BR-1: Uniform Authorization Model (No Tiering)
    14→
    15→- ALL Brain operations -- reads, writes, integrations -- require RAR authorization with structured `brain_action` authorization_details
    16→- There is NO distinction between "consequential" and "non-consequential" operations at the authorization layer
    17→- Classification is a vulnerability: any tier boundary is an attack surface. The system has zero tier boundaries.
    18→- The Authorizer Agent evaluates Rich Intent Objects only. It never evaluates scopes.
    19→
    20→### BR-2: Intent-Token Binding
    21→
    22→- Every RAR access token MUST reference a valid, authorized intent via `urn:brain:intent_id` claim
    23→- The token's `authorization_details` MUST match the intent's `brain_action` (or its human-constrained variant)
    24→- No RAR token can be issued without an authorized intent -- the Custom AS remains the authorization decision point
    25→
    26→### BR-3: Sender Constraining (Universal)
    27→
    28→- DPoP-bound tokens are cryptographically tied to the actor's key pair via `cnf.jkt` claim
    29→- The actor's DPoP public key thumbprint is registered with the intent at submission time
    30→- Only the holder of the matching private key can present the token to the Brain
    31→- Private keys MUST NOT leave the actor's runtime boundary (sandbox, browser, CLI process)
    32→- Both agents AND humans have DPoP key pairs
    33→
    34→### BR-4: Human Oversight (Unchanged Trigger, Uniform Mechanism)
    35→
    36→- Actions entering `veto_window` (risk_score > 30, decision = APPROVE) present RAR `authorization_details` to the human operator in human-readable form
    37→- Humans can approve, constrain (reduce scope/budget), or veto
    38→- Constraints modify the `authorization_details` that flow into the issued token
    39→- Veto window expiry (default: 30 minutes) auto-authorizes medium-risk actions unless explicitly vetoed
    40→- The risk router determines WHICH operations need human review, NOT which operations use RAR (all do)
    41→
    42→### BR-5: Replay Protection
    43→
    44→- Each DPoP proof MUST contain a unique `jti` nonce
    45→- The Brain resource server maintains a time-windowed nonce cache (entries auto-expire after clock skew window)
    46→- Reused `jti` values are rejected with a specific error code
    47→- The nonce cache MUST be scoped per Brain resource server instance (injected via dependency injection, not module-level singleton)
    48→- Nonce cache must be sized for ALL request volume (not just "consequential" operations)
    49→
    50→### BR-6: Session-Brain Separation (The Bridge)
    51→
    52→- Better Auth sessions CANNOT directly access Brain resources
    53→- Session cookies presented to the Brain resource server are rejected with 401 "dpop_required"
    54→- Bearer tokens presented to the Brain resource server are rejected with 401 "dpop_required"
    55→- Humans must exchange their Better Auth session for a DPoP-bound RAR token via the Bridge endpoint
    56→- The Custom AS validates the Better Auth session is active before issuing a Bridge token
    57→- Better Auth scopes (e.g., "dashboard:access") are consumed by the dashboard UI only, never by the Brain
    58→
    59→### BR-7: Human Parity
    60→
    61→- Human and agent tokens are verified identically at the Brain boundary
    62→- The Brain resource server does not differentiate verification logic based on actor type
    63→- Audit trail entries use the same `brain_action` format for both human and agent operations
    64→- If Marcus wants to create an invoice, he generates a DPoP-signed intent just like agent Kira
    65→
    66→### BR-8: Better Auth as Proxy IdP
    67→
    68→- Human logs into dashboard via Better Auth (standard session)
    69→- Human can create "Managed Agent" identity nodes
    70→- Custom AS records `identity:agent_123` is `managed_by` Better Auth `userId:human_456`
    71→- When agent requests token, Custom AS can check Better Auth to verify managing human is still active
    72→- Better Auth = "Soul" (human identity). Custom AS = "Skeleton" (actor tokens + DPoP).
    73→
    74→## Functional Requirements
    75→
    76→### FR-1: DPoP Key Pair Management (All Actors)
    77→
    78→- Agent runtimes generate ES256 key pairs within their sandbox
    79→- Dashboard clients generate ES256 key pairs in browser memory (Web Crypto API)
    80→- CLI tools generate ES256 key pairs in process memory
    81→- Key pairs persist for the duration of the actor session (not per-operation)
    82→- JWK thumbprint (RFC 7638) computed from the public key is the binding identifier
    83→- Key pair lifecycle: generated at session start, destroyed at runtime termination
    84→
    85→### FR-2: Intent Submission with DPoP Binding
    86→
    87→- Extend the intent submission endpoint to accept `dpop_jwk_thumbprint`
    88→- The thumbprint links the intent to the actor's key pair before token issuance
    89→- ALL Brain operations require intent submission -- reads, writes, integrations
    90→- The evaluation pipeline (Policy Gate -> Authorizer Agent -> Risk Router -> Status Update) processes every intent
    91→- Low-risk operations (reads, observations) auto-approve through the pipeline without human involvement
    92→
    93→### FR-3: RAR Token Endpoint (Custom AS)
    94→
    95→- New grant type: `urn:brain:intent-authorization`
    96→- Accepts `intent_id` and `authorization_details` parameters
    97→- Validates DPoP proof header (structure, signature, freshness)
    98→- Verifies intent exists, is authorized, and requestor identity matches
    99→- Verifies authorization_details matches intent brain_action
   100→- Verifies DPoP proof key thumbprint matches intent dpop_jwk_thumbprint
   101→- Issues DPoP-bound access token with `cnf.jkt`, `authorization_details`, `urn:brain:intent_id` claims
   102→- Token TTL: configurable per operation risk level (default: 300 seconds)
   103→
   104→### FR-4: Bridge Token Exchange Endpoint
   105→
   106→- New endpoint: `POST /api/auth/bridge/exchange`
   107→- Accepts Better Auth session cookie + DPoP proof + `authorization_details`
   108→- Validates Better Auth session is still active (API call to Better Auth)
   109→- Resolves human identity from session
   110→- Authorizer Agent evaluates the brain_action (same pipeline as agent intents)
   111→- Issues DPoP-bound access token with brain_action authorization_details
   112→- Low-risk reads auto-approve (no human consent needed for the human's own reads)
   113→- High-risk operations from the Bridge follow the same veto window path
   114→
   115→### FR-5: RAR Consent Rendering
   116→
   117→- Transform `authorization_details` JSON into human-readable consent display
   118→- Provider-specific rendering rules (e.g., Stripe amounts in dollars, not cents)
   119→- Display risk score, Authorizer Agent reasoning, and veto window expiry
   120→- Support approve, constrain, and veto actions
   121→- Constrain action produces modified authorization_details with tighter bounds
   122→
   123→### FR-6: DPoP Proof Verification at Brain Resource Server
   124→
   125→- Extract and validate DPoP proof from request headers
   126→- Reject Bearer tokens, session cookies, and missing Authorization headers with "dpop_required"
   127→- Verify proof structure (typ, alg, jwk), signature, and claims (htm, htu, iat, jti)
   128→- Compute JWK thumbprint and compare against access token `cnf.jkt`
   129→- Check `jti` against nonce cache for replay protection
   130→- Clock skew tolerance: configurable, default 60 seconds past, 5 seconds future
   131→- ONE verification pipeline for ALL requests -- no branching on actor type
   132→
   133→### FR-7: RAR Operation Scope Verification at Brain Resource Server
   134→
   135→- Extract requested operation from API route and request body as a `brain_action`
   136→- Match against access token `authorization_details`
   137→- Type must be "brain_action" (always)
   138→- Action and resource must match exactly
   139→- Request constraints must be within authorized bounds
   140→- Tokens without authorization_details are rejected (no scope fallback)
   141→
   142→### FR-8: Audit Trail (Uniform)
   143→
   144→- Every intent submission, evaluation, routing decision, human consent action, token issuance, and Brain resource server verification is logged
   145→- Audit entries link to intent_id and include DPoP JWK thumbprint
   146→- Security events (thumbprint mismatch, replay detection) are logged at elevated severity
   147→- Human and agent operations use the same audit format -- `brain_action` entries
   148→
   149→## Non-Functional Requirements
   150→
   151→### NFR-1: Performance
   152→
   153→- DPoP key pair generation: < 50ms per session (agent sandbox or browser)
   154→- DPoP proof construction: < 5ms per request
   155→- Token endpoint response time: < 200ms (excluding intent evaluation, which is async)
   156→- Bridge exchange response time: < 300ms (includes Better Auth session validation)
   157→- DPoP proof verification at Brain resource server: < 10ms per request
   158→- Nonce cache lookup: < 1ms
   159→
   160→### NFR-2: Security
   161→
   162→- Private keys never leave the actor's runtime boundary (enforced by runtime isolation)
   163→- DPoP proofs are single-use (replay protection via nonce cache)
   164→- Access tokens have short TTL (300 seconds default, configurable)
   165→- Clock skew tolerance is configurable but defaults to conservative values
   166→- All verification failures produce specific, actionable error codes (not generic 401)
   167→- Session cookies cannot access the Brain (enforced at Brain boundary)
   168→- No scope fallback at the Brain (enforced at Brain boundary)
   169→
   170→### NFR-3: Reliability
   171→
   172→- Authorizer Agent timeout (30 seconds) falls back to APPROVE with risk_score=50, routing to veto_window (existing behavior preserved)
   173→- Nonce cache entries auto-expire (no unbounded memory growth)
   174→- Token re-issuance is allowed within intent expiry window if original token expires before use
   175→- Better Auth session expiry during Bridge exchange returns clear error with redirect guidance
   176→
   177→### NFR-4: Compatibility
   178→
   179→- TypeScript/Bun runtime
   180→- SurrealDB SCHEMAFULL mode for all new tables
   181→- Functional paradigm: pure functions, pipeline composition, no classes
   182→- No `null` in domain data -- use `undefined` via optional properties
   183→- No module-level mutable singletons -- nonce cache and key stores injected via dependency injection
   184→- Better Auth IdP integration preserved for human login -- Custom AS is a separate service/layer
   185→
   186→## Dependencies
   187→
   188→| Dependency | Status | Impact |
   189→|---|---|---|
   190→| Better Auth IdP (existing) | Available | Human login, session management, Proxy IdP for Bridge |
   191→| Intent Node (existing) | Available | Evaluation pipeline, risk router |
   192→| Unified Identity (existing) | Available | Identity resolution for token claims |
   193→| Authority System (existing) | Available | Permission matrix for intent lifecycle actions |
   194→| jose library (existing) | Available | JWT signing/verification, JWKS, JWK thumbprint |
   195→| SurrealDB (existing) | Available | Schema extension for DPoP binding fields, brain_action types |
   196→| Web Crypto API (Bun built-in) | Available | ES256 key generation for agent runtimes |
   197→| Web Crypto API (browser) | Available | ES256 key generation for dashboard Bridge |
   198→
   199→## Risks
   200→
   201→| Risk | Probability | Impact | Mitigation |
   202→|---|---|---|---|
   203→| Clock skew between actor runtimes and Brain resource server | Medium | Medium | Configurable tolerance window, NTP requirement, actionable error messages |
   204→| Nonce cache memory growth for ALL requests (not just writes) | Medium | Medium | Time-windowed set with aggressive TTLs, dependency-injected per instance |
   205→| DPoP complexity discouraging agent and dashboard developers | Medium | High | Client libraries abstract key management and proof construction for both agent and browser |
   206→| Bridge latency perceived by dashboard users | Medium | Medium | Token caching for common read patterns (60s TTL), batch token requests for dashboard initialization |
   207→| Browser DPoP key management compatibility | Low | Medium | Web Crypto API is widely supported; fallback is not possible -- security is non-negotiable |
   208→| Better Auth plugin compatibility with Bridge endpoint | Low | High | Spike to validate Custom AS can validate Better Auth sessions via API |
   209→| Developer resistance to "session cookie cannot access API" paradigm | High | Medium | Clear documentation, client library that makes Bridge transparent, emphasize security model |
   210→

1→# User Stories: OAuth RAR + DPoP (Sovereign Hybrid Model)
     2→
     3→---
     4→
     5→## US-001: DPoP Key Pair Lifecycle for All Actors
     6→
     7→### Problem
     8→
     9→Agent "Kira" (code_agent in E2B sandbox, workspace "Lusaka") and human operator Marcus Santos (dashboard user) currently have no sender-constraining mechanism. Kira authenticates with a long-lived Bearer token that any interceptor can replay. Marcus authenticates with a session cookie that any XSS attack can steal. Neither can prove that a token presentation came from the same entity the token was issued to.
    10→
    11→### Who
    12→
    13→- AI agent runtime | Running in isolated sandbox (E2B) | Needs cryptographic identity for proof-of-possession
    14→- Human operator | Using dashboard in browser | Needs browser-based cryptographic identity for Bridge tokens
    15→
    16→### Solution
    17→
    18→All actor runtimes (agent sandboxes, browser dashboard, CLI tools) generate and manage an ES256 key pair per session, computing a JWK thumbprint that serves as the binding identifier for all DPoP-bound tokens in that session.
    19→
    20→### Domain Examples
    21→
